tell me how to build, test, profile for AX45MPV cpu with RVV vlen 1024 
using toolchain /local/nick/SW_Release_cp/ast530/nds64le-elf-newlib-v5d/bin/
qemu /local/nick/qemu_andes/build/qemu-system-riscv64 and board sw-boards.andestech.com:1116 
 QEMU: Andes fork of QEMU 9.2.0 at /local/nick/qemu_andes/build/qemu-system-riscv64 — supports Andes
  CPU models like andes-ax45mpv with configurable VLEN. In Claude Code's sandbox, semihosting output
  goes to fd 9 instead of stdout

#!/usr/bin/env bash
################################################################################
#Input:
# $1: CPU name (case insensitive)
#       this could be N10/D10/D15/N225/D23/N25/NX25/D25/AX25/A27/AX27/N45/D45/NX45/AX45/NX27V
# $2: Extra compilation flags for the library
#       if this parameter is set, these extra flags will
#       be appended to the default compilation flags
#       ; otherwise, the default compilation flags
#       defined in this script will be applied.
# $3: Specified compiler
#       If this parameter is left to be empty, the
#       nds32le-elf-gcc (for V3),
#       riscv32-elf-gcc (for RV32),
#       riscv64-elf-gcc (for RV64) will be used as default compiler.
# $4: Library output path
#       As thsi parameter is set, the built library and objects will be saved
#       into the user specified path; otherwise, the built library and
#       objects will be saved in the root of this repository.
#
# Example:
#   ./build.sh D10
# Or
#   ./build.sh D10 "-funroll-loops --param max-unroll-times=2"
# Or
#   ./build.sh D25 "" riscv32-linux-gcc
# Or
#   ./build.sh D25 "" riscv32-elf-clang
###############################################################################

#----- build switches -----
BUILD_LIBNN=1
BUILD_DOC=0
MAKE_INSTALL=1

# common compilation flags
CFLAG="-Wall -Werror -O3 -ffunction-sections -fdata-sections"

#convert CPU name to lower case
CPU=$(echo ${1} | tr A-Z a-z)

#set the default compilation flags
if [ "${CPU}" == "n10" ]; then
    CFLAG="${CFLAG} -mcpu=n10 -std=c99"
    TOOLCHAIN_PREFIX="nds32le-elf-"
elif [ "${CPU}" == "d10" ]; then
    CFLAG="${CFLAG} -mcpu=d10 -mext-dsp -mext-zol -std=c99"
    TOOLCHAIN_PREFIX="nds32le-elf-"
elif [ "${CPU}" == "d15" ]; then
    CFLAG="${CFLAG} -mcpu=d15 -mext-dsp -mext-zol -funroll-loops --param max-unroll-times=4 -fno-auto-inc-dec -std=c99"
    TOOLCHAIN_PREFIX="nds32le-elf-"
elif [ "${CPU}" == "n225" ]; then
    CFLAG="${CFLAG} -mtune=andes-25-series -fno-strict-aliasing -mext-zc"
    TOOLCHAIN_PREFIX="riscv32-elf-"
elif [ "${CPU}" == "d23" ]; then
    CFLAG="${CFLAG} -mtune=andes-23-series -mext-dsp -fno-strict-aliasing -mext-zc"
    TOOLCHAIN_PREFIX="riscv32-elf-"
elif [ "${CPU}" == "n25" ]; then
    CFLAG="${CFLAG} -mtune=andes-25-series -fno-strict-aliasing"
    TOOLCHAIN_PREFIX="riscv32-elf-"
elif [ "${CPU}" == "nx25" ]; then
    CFLAG="${CFLAG} -mtune=andes-25-series -fno-strict-aliasing"
    TOOLCHAIN_PREFIX="riscv64-elf-"
elif [ "${CPU}" == "d25" ] || [ "${CPU}" == "a25" ]; then
    CFLAG="${CFLAG} -mtune=andes-25-series -mext-dsp -fno-strict-aliasing"
    TOOLCHAIN_PREFIX="riscv32-elf-"
elif [ "${CPU}" == "dx25" ] || [ "${CPU}" == "ax25" ]; then
    CFLAG="${CFLAG} -mtune=andes-25-series -mext-dsp -fno-strict-aliasing"
    TOOLCHAIN_PREFIX="riscv64-elf-"
elif [ "${CPU}" == "a27" ]; then
    CFLAG="${CFLAG} -mtune=andes-27-series -mext-dsp -fno-strict-aliasing"
    TOOLCHAIN_PREFIX="riscv32-elf-"
elif [ "${CPU}" == "ax27" ]; then
    CFLAG="${CFLAG} -mtune=andes-27-series -mext-dsp -fno-strict-aliasing"
    TOOLCHAIN_PREFIX="riscv64-elf-"
elif [ "${CPU}" == "nx25v_freya" ]; then
    CFLAG="${CFLAG} -fno-strict-aliasing -mext-vector -DENA_VEC_ISA -DMAX_VLEN=512 -DNDS_VEC_RVV_VERSION=80"
    TOOLCHAIN_PREFIX="riscv64-elf-"
elif [ "${CPU}" == "nx27v" ]; then
    CFLAG="${CFLAG} -fno-strict-aliasing -mext-vector -DENA_VEC_ISA -DMAX_VLEN=1024 -DNDS_VEC_RVV_VERSION=1000 -DENA_NDS_V5_VEC_DOT_PROD -fno-tree-slp-vectorize -fno-tree-vectorize"
    TOOLCHAIN_PREFIX="riscv64-elf-"
elif [ "${CPU}" == "n45" ]; then
    CFLAG="${CFLAG} -mtune=andes-45-series -fno-strict-aliasing"
    TOOLCHAIN_PREFIX="riscv32-elf-"
elif [ "${CPU}" == "d45" ] || [ "${CPU}" == "a45" ]; then
    CFLAG="${CFLAG} -mtune=andes-45-series -mext-dsp -fno-strict-aliasing"
    TOOLCHAIN_PREFIX="riscv32-elf-"
elif [ "${CPU}" == "nx45" ]; then
    CFLAG="${CFLAG} -mtune=andes-45-series -fno-strict-aliasing"
    TOOLCHAIN_PREFIX="riscv64-elf-"
elif [ "${CPU}" == "ax45" ]; then
    CFLAG="${CFLAG} -mtune=andes-45-series -mext-dsp -fno-strict-aliasing"
    TOOLCHAIN_PREFIX="riscv64-elf-"
elif [ "${CPU}" == "ax45mpv" ]; then
    CFLAG="${CFLAG} -mtune=andes-45-series -fno-strict-aliasing -mext-vector -DENA_VEC_ISA -DMAX_VLEN=1024 -DNDS_VEC_RVV_VERSION=1000 -DENA_NDS_V5_VEC_DOT_PROD -fno-tree-slp-vectorize -fno-tree-vectorize"
    TOOLCHAIN_PREFIX="riscv64-elf-"
elif [ "${CPU}" == "ax45mpv_ace" ]; then
    CFLAG="${CFLAG} -mtune=andes-45-series -fno-strict-aliasing -mext-vector -DENA_VEC_ISA -DMAX_VLEN=1024 -DNDS_VEC_RVV_VERSION=1000 -DENA_NDS_V5_VEC_DOT_PROD -fno-tree-slp-vectorize -fno-tree-vectorize -DENA_ACE_RVV -Wa,-mace=${PWD}/ace_lib_exp/libacetool.so"
    TOOLCHAIN_PREFIX="riscv64-elf-"
elif [ "${CPU}" == "a46mp" ]; then

#!/bin/bash

#==================== controlling switches (begin) ====================
RUN_MODE=0          # the mode used to run QEMU
                    #  - 0: standalone mode
                    #  - 1: GDB server mode
#==================== controlling switches (end)   ====================

OLD_PWD=`pwd`
ADX_PATH="$( cd "$( dirname "$1" )" && echo `pwd`/$(basename "$1"))"
THIS_DIR="$( cd "$( dirname "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}" )" && pwd )"

# this value should be replaced by build system when start testing
SIMULATOR_DIR=${THIS_DIR}

# QEMU configs
QEMU=${QEMU_SYS_RV64}
CPU=andes-ax45mpv,vext_spec=v1.0,vlen=1024
if [[ -z ${VEC_CFG} ]]; then
    # by default, select the zve64d config
    CONFIG=${SIMULATOR_DIR}/qemu_cfg/zve64d/ADP-AE350-AX45MPV-1C_dw0_mw1.cfg
elif [[ ! ${VEC_CFG} =~ ^zve32(x|f)$ ]] && [[ ! ${VEC_CFG} =~ ^zve64(x|f|d)$ ]]; then
    # for the case of using unsupported VEC_CFG
    echo "Warning! The VEC_CFF=${VEC_CFG} is not a supported vector configuration. Select the default vector configuration (zve64d) for QEMU."
    CONFIG=${SIMULATOR_DIR}/qemu_cfg/zve64d/ADP-AE350-AX45MPV-1C_dw0_mw1.cfg
else
    CONFIG=${SIMULATOR_DIR}/qemu_cfg/${VEC_CFG}/ADP-AE350-AX45MPV-1C_dw0_mw1.cfg
fi

# check if the execution file is available
if [ "$1" = "" ] || [ ! -f "$1" ]; then
    echo ""
    echo "No such file $1"
    echo ""
    exit 1
fi

# get the executable file
RUN_FILE=$ADX_PATH

# Run qemu
EXTRA_QEMU_CONF=""
if [[ ${RUN_MODE} -eq 1 ]]; then
    EXTRA_QEMU_CONF="-S -s"
fi
$QEMU -readconfig $CONFIG -M andes_ae350 -cpu $CPU -nographic -semihosting -bios $RUN_FILE ${EXTRA_QEMU_CONF} 2>&1

ret=$?
cd ${OLD_PWD}
exit ${ret}

1→#!/usr/bin/env bash
     2→################################################################################
     3→#
     4→# This script is used to verify libnn accuracy, code coverage and measure
     5→# performance.
     6→#
     7→# Input:
     8→#   $1: Source code path (without / tailed)
     9→#       This is a dummy argument, just for making the API be the same as test.sh
    10→#       of libdsp.
    11→#   $2: Compiler name (with prefix)
    12→#       Set it to the compiler name without full path, e.g., nds32le-elf-gcc,
    13→#       riscv32-elf-clang, riscv64-linux-gcc, etc. This script will try to find
    14→#       the full path of this specified compiler from the PATH environment
    15→#       variable; therefore, please export the proper toolcahin path into PATH
    16→#       environment variable before using this script.
    17→#   $3: Simulator name
    18→#       In DAILY test, please give the full path of simulator.
    19→#   $4: Simulation target
    20→#       It could be GDB, SID or BOARD.
    21→#   $5: BS3 / DAILY
    22→#       BS3:   Verify the accuracy with the built-in multi-lib. (note the sat
    23→#              extension and code coverage won't be verified here)
    24→#       DAILY: Use the user specified compiler and compilation options to build
    25→#              the library, and this built library will be used in the accuracy
    26→#              tests later. Not only the accuracy but also the code coverage
    27→#              will be verified in this mode.
    28→#   $6: Extra flags for selecting multi-lib or the algorithm to be built as library
    29→#       As the 5th argument is set to BS3, these flags are used to select the
    30→#       built-in multi-lib. For example, if "-mext-dsp" is contained in this
    31→#       argument, the DSP version library will be used for linking. By default,
    32→#       the plain C version library will be used for linking.
    33→#       As the 5th argument is set to DAILY, these flags will be used to select
    34→#       the proper algorithm and build them into library. For example, if
    35→#       "-mext-dsp" is contained in this argument, the DSP algorithm will be
    36→#       used as building the library. By default, the plain C algorithm will be
    37→#       used to build the library.
    38→#   $7: Specified linker
    39→#       Use the specified linker instead of the default one when building the
    40→#       executable. If this argument is left to be empty, the default linker
    41→#       will be used. (e.g., ld, lld, etc.)
    42→#
    43→# Example:
    44→#   mkdir build_dir && cd build_dir
    45→#
    46→#   Ex-1: use default linker then test plain C algorithm with V3 GDB run
    47→#   ../test.sh "" nds32le-elf-gcc nds32le-elf-run GDB BS3
    48→#
    49→#   Ex-2: use default linker then test DSP algorithm with RV32 SID
    50→#   ../test.sh "" riscv32-elf-gcc riscv-sim-wrapper SID BS3 "-mext-dsp"
    51→#
    52→#   Ex-3: use lld as linker, clang as compiler then test DSP algorithm with RV64 SID
    53→#   ../test.sh "" riscv64-linux-clang riscv-sim-wrapper SID BS3 "-mext-dsp" lld
    54→#
    55→#   Ex-4: use default linker then test v-ext 0.8 algorithm with SID
    56→#   ../test.sh "" riscv64-elf-gcc riscv-sim-wrapper SID BS3 "-mext-vector -DNDS_VEC_RVV_VERSION=80"
    57→#
    58→#   Ex-5: use default linker then test v-ext 1.0 algorithm with SID

    92→                    fun_name=$(grep -w "Function" <<< ${called_fun} | cut -d "'" -f2)

    97→                    fi
    98→                fi

   100→                called_fun_list=("${called_fun_list[@]/${called_fun}}")

#!/usr/bin/env bash
################################################################################
#
# This script is used to verify libnn accuracy, code coverage and measure
# performance.
#
# Input:
#   $1: Source code path (without / tailed)
#       This is a dummy argument, just for making the API be the same as test.sh
#       of libdsp.
#   $2: Compiler name (with prefix)
#       Set it to the compiler name without full path, e.g., nds32le-elf-gcc,
#       riscv32-elf-clang, riscv64-linux-gcc, etc. This script will try to find
#       the full path of this specified compiler from the PATH environment
#       variable; therefore, please export the proper toolcahin path into PATH
#       environment variable before using this script.
#   $3: Simulator name
#       In DAILY test, please give the full path of simulator.
#   $4: Simulation target
#       It could be GDB, SID or BOARD.
#   $5: BS3 / DAILY
#       BS3:   Verify the accuracy with the built-in multi-lib. (note the sat
#              extension and code coverage won't be verified here)
#       DAILY: Use the user specified compiler and compilation options to build
#              the library, and this built library will be used in the accuracy
#              tests later. Not only the accuracy but also the code coverage
#              will be verified in this mode.
#   $6: Extra flags for selecting multi-lib or the algorithm to be built as library
#       As the 5th argument is set to BS3, these flags are used to select the
#       built-in multi-lib. For example, if "-mext-dsp" is contained in this
#       argument, the DSP version library will be used for linking. By default,
#       the plain C version library will be used for linking.
#       As the 5th argument is set to DAILY, these flags will be used to select
#       the proper algorithm and build them into library. For example, if
#       "-mext-dsp" is contained in this argument, the DSP algorithm will be
#       used as building the library. By default, the plain C algorithm will be
#       used to build the library.
#   $7: Specified linker
#       Use the specified linker instead of the default one when building the
#       executable. If this argument is left to be empty, the default linker
#       will be used. (e.g., ld, lld, etc.)
#
# Example:
#   mkdir build_dir && cd build_dir
#
#   Ex-1: use default linker then test plain C algorithm with V3 GDB run
#   ../test.sh "" nds32le-elf-gcc nds32le-elf-run GDB BS3
#
#   Ex-2: use default linker then test DSP algorithm with RV32 SID
#   ../test.sh "" riscv32-elf-gcc riscv-sim-wrapper SID BS3 "-mext-dsp"
#
#   Ex-3: use lld as linker, clang as compiler then test DSP algorithm with RV64 SID
#   ../test.sh "" riscv64-linux-clang riscv-sim-wrapper SID BS3 "-mext-dsp" lld
#
#   Ex-4: use default linker then test v-ext 0.8 algorithm with SID
#   ../test.sh "" riscv64-elf-gcc riscv-sim-wrapper SID BS3 "-mext-vector -DNDS_VEC_RVV_VERSION=80"
#
#   Ex-5: use default linker then test v-ext 1.0 algorithm with SID
#   ../test.sh "" riscv64-elf-gcc riscv-sim-wrapper SID BS3 "-mext-vector -DNDS_VEC_RVV_VERSION=1000"
#
# Note:
#   In build-system-3, the 3rd parameter is allways "riscv-sim-wrapper".
#   build-system-3 will prepare a proper warpper named "riscv-sim-wrapper"
#   according to the specified simulation target by users, and this wrapper will
#   be used in the accuray test later.
#
################################################################################

#######################################################
# check the coverage of executed functions
#######################################################
function check_coverage {
    local gcda_list called_fun_list fun_coverage fun_name coverage_status
    # remember change directory to the folder used to keep gcov outputs
    cd ${GCOV_DIR}

    gcda_list=($(ls ${LIB_OBJ_DIR}/*.gcda))
    for gcda in ${gcda_list[@]}; do
        called_fun_list=$(${GCOV} -f ${gcda} | grep -w "Function" -A1)
        IFS=-
        called_fun_list=(${called_fun_list})
        IFS=
        coverage_status=""
        is_coverage_fail=0

        # let the function with riscv_nn prefix be shown first
        for called_fun in "${called_fun_list[@]}"; do
            if [[ "${called_fun}" == *"${FUN_PREFIX}"* ]]; then
                fun_coverage=$(grep -Eo "[[:digit:]]{1,3}.[[:digit:]]{2}%" <<< ${called_fun})
                # remember to ignore those functions with 0.00% coverage
                if [[ "${fun_coverage}" != 0.00% ]]; then
                    fun_name=$(grep -w "Function" <<< ${called_fun} | cut -d "'" -f2)
                    coverage_status+="${fun_name},${fun_coverage},"
                    # coverage_status+=""
                    if [[ "${fun_coverage}" != "100.00%" ]]; then
                        is_coverage_fail=1
                    fi
                fi

                called_fun_list=("${called_fun_list[@]/${called_fun}}")
                break
            fi
        done

        for called_fun in ${called_fun_list[@]}; do
            fun_coverage=$(grep -Eo "[[:digit:]]{1,3}.[[:digit:]]{2}%" <<< ${called_fun})
            # remember to ignore those functions with 0.00% coverage
            if [[ "${fun_coverage}" != 0.00% ]]; then
                fun_name=$(grep -w "Function" <<< ${called_fun} | cut -d "'" -f2)
                coverage_status+="${fun_name},${fun_coverage},"
                if [[ "${fun_coverage}" != "100.00%" ]]; then
                    is_coverage_fail=1
                fi
            fi
        done

        if [ ${is_coverage_fail} -eq 1 ]; then
            coverage_fail_list+=("${coverage_status}")
            echo ${coverage_status} >> ${COVERAGE_FAIL_LIST}
            cp $(basename ${gcda/gcda/c.gcov}) ${FAILED_GCOV_DIR}

#!/usr/bin/env bash
###########################################################################
# This script is used to collect the libnn functions' performance with the
# built-in libraries.
#
# Input:
#  $1: CPU name
#      supported CPU list:
#      (1) CPU w/o VPU:      n225, d23, n25, a25, d25, nx25, ax25, a27, n45,
#                            a45, d45, nx45, ax45, a46mp, ax46mp, ax65
#      (2) 27-series w/ VPU: nx27v, nx27v_elen64, nx27v_elen64_felen64,
#                            nx27v_purec
#      (3) 45-series w/ VPU: ax45mpv, ax45mpv_elen64, ax45mpv_elen64_felen64,
#                            ax45mpv_seg, ax45mpv_elen64_seg,
#                            ax45mpv_elen64_felen64_seg, ax45mpv_purec
#      (4) 46-series w/ VPU: a46mpv, a46mpv_seg, a46mpv_purec, ax46mpv,
#                            ax46mpv_elen64, ax46mpv_elen64_felen64,
#                            ax46mpv_seg, ax46mpv_elen64_seg,
#                            ax46mpv_elen64_felen64_seg, ax46mpv_purec
#      (5) 60-series w/ VPU: ax66, ax66_elen64, ax66_elen64_felen64, ax66_seg,
#                            ax66_elen64_seg, ax66_elen64_felen64_seg,
#                            ax66_purec
#  $2: compiler name
#      Now only the elf toolchains are supported and the supported compilers
#      are:
#        - riscv32-elf-gcc
#        - riscv32-elf-clang
#        - riscv64-elf-gcc
#        - riscv64-elf-clang
#  $3: the target to run the executables
#      Now the target could only be BOARD.
#
# Example:
#  ex-1: measure the performance on FPGA with D25 bitmap and clang compiler
#  ./test_perf.sh "d25" "riscv32-elf-gcc" "BOARD"
#
#  ex-2: measure the performance on FPGA with AX25 bitmap and clang compiler
#  ./test_perf.sh "ax25" "riscv64-elf-clang" "BOARD"
#
#  ex-3: measure the performance on FPGA with NX27V bitmap and gcc compiler
#  ./test_perf.sh "nx27v" "riscv64-elf-gcc" "BOARD"
#
#  ex-4: measure the pure C algorithm performance on FPGA with NX27V bitmap
#  and clang compiler
#  ./test_perf.sh "nx27v_purec" "riscv64-elf-clang" "BOARD"
###########################################################################

CPU_NAME=${1,,}   ## always convert to lower case
COMPILER_NAME="$2"
if [ -z ${3} ]; then
    TARGET="BOARD"
else
    TARGET="${3}"
fi

function log () {
    echo "$*" | tee -a ${TEST_LOG}
}

##### misc settings #####
LIB_NAME="libnn"
LIB_ROOT=$(readlink -f `dirname ${0}`)
TEST_LOG="test_perf.log"
ERROR_LOG="diff_log"

# set the proper cflag accrording to the CPU_NAME
if [[ ${CPU_NAME} == n225 ]]; then
    CPU_FLAGS="-mext-zc"
elif [[ ${CPU_NAME} == d23 ]]; then
    CPU_FLAGS="-mext-dsp -mext-zc"
elif [[ ${CPU_NAME} =~ ^n(x)?25$ ]]; then
    CPU_FLAGS=""
elif [[ ${CPU_NAME} =~ ^(a|d|ax)25$ ]] ||
     [[ ${CPU_NAME} =~ ^a27$ ]]; then
    CPU_FLAGS="-mext-dsp"
elif [[ ${CPU_NAME} =~ ^n(x)?45$ ]]; then
    CPU_FLAGS="-mtune=andes-45-series"
elif [[ ${CPU_NAME} =~ ^(a|d|ax)45$ ]]; then
    CPU_FLAGS="-mtune=andes-45-series -mext-dsp"
elif [[ ${CPU_NAME} =~ ^(a|ax)46mp$ ]]; then
    CPU_FLAGS="-mtune=andes-45-series -mext-dsp -mext-zc"
elif [[ ${CPU_NAME} == ax65 ]]; then
    CPU_FLAGS="-mtune=andes-60-series -DENA_RUN_TWICE -DENA_CACHEABLE_ENV_SETUP"
elif [[ ${CPU_NAME} =~ ^nx27v(_elen64|_elen64_felen64)?$ ]]; then
    CPU_FLAGS="-mext-vector -DNDS_VEC_RVV_VERSION=1000 -DENA_RUN_TWICE -DENA_CACHEABLE_ENV_SETUP"
elif [[ ${CPU_NAME} =~ ^ax45mpv(_elen64(_felen64)?(_seg)?|_seg)?$ ]]; then
    CPU_FLAGS="-mtune=andes-45-series -mext-vector -DNDS_VEC_RVV_VERSION=1000 -DENA_RUN_TWICE -DENA_CACHEABLE_ENV_SETUP"
elif [[ ${CPU_NAME} =~ ^a46mpv(_seg)?$ ]]; then
    ${COMPILER_NAME} -E -dM - < /dev/null | grep -qw "__riscv_float_abi_soft"
    is_soft_ft_abi=$?
    if [[ ${is_soft_ft_abi} -eq 0 ]]; then
        VEC_CFG="zve32x"
    else
        VEC_CFG="zve32f"
    fi

    CPU_FLAGS="-mtune=andes-45-series -mext-vector=${VEC_CFG} -mext-zc -DNDS_VEC_RVV_VERSION=1000 -DENA_RUN_TWICE -DENA_CACHEABLE_ENV_SETUP"
elif [[ ${CPU_NAME} =~ ^ax46mpv(_elen64(_felen64)?(_seg)?|_seg?)?$ ]]; then
    CPU_FLAGS="-mtune=andes-45-series -mext-vector -mext-zc -DNDS_VEC_RVV_VERSION=1000 -DENA_RUN_TWICE -DENA_CACHEABLE_ENV_SETUP"
elif [[ ${CPU_NAME} =~ ^ax66(_elen64(_felen64)?(_seg)?|_seg)?$ ]]; then
    CPU_FLAGS="-mtune=andes-60-series -mext-vector -DNDS_VEC_RVV_VERSION=1000 -DENA_RUN_TWICE -DENA_CACHEABLE_ENV_SETUP"
elif [[ ${CPU_NAME} =~ ^(nx27v|ax45mpv)_purec$ ]]; then
    CPU_FLAGS="-DENA_RUN_TWICE -DENA_CACHEABLE_ENV_SETUP"
elif [[ ${CPU_NAME} =~ ^(a|ax)46mpv_purec$ ]]; then
    CPU_FLAGS="-DENA_RUN_TWICE -DENA_CACHEABLE_ENV_SETUP -mext-zc"
elif [[ ${CPU_NAME} == ax66_purec ]]; then
    CPU_FLAGS="-mtune=andes-60-series -DENA_RUN_TWICE -DENA_CACHEABLE_ENV_SETUP"
else
    echo "Error! ${1} is not a supported CPU !"
    echo "Supported CPUs are listed below:
- CPU w/o VPU: n225, d23, n25, a25, d25, nx25, ax25, a27, n45, a45, d45, nx45, ax45, a46mp, ax46mp, ax65
- 27-series w/ VPU: nx27v, nx27v_elen64, nx27v_elen64_felen64, nx27v_purec
- 45-series w/ VPU: ax45mpv, ax45mpv_elen64, ax45mpv_elen64_felen64, ax45mpv_seg, ax45mpv_elen64_seg, ax45mpv_elen64_felen64_seg, ax45mpv_purec
- 46-series w/ VPU: a46mpv, a46mpv_seg, a46mpv_purec, ax46mpv, ax46mpv_elen64, ax46mpv_elen64_felen64, ax46mpv_seg, ax46mpv_elen64_seg, ax46mpv_elen64_felen64_seg, ax46mpv_purec
- 60-series w/ VPU: ax66, ax66_elen64, ax66_elen64_felen64, ax66_seg, ax66_elen64_seg, ax66_elen64_felen64_seg, ax66_purec"

    touch "../${ERROR_LOG}"
    exit 1
fi

    31→ENA_VL4=1           #0: disable VL4 extension, 1: enable VL4 extension
    32→VEC_CONFIG=4        #0: zve32x, 1: zve32f, 2: zve64x, 3: zve64f, 4: zv64d
    33→

    35→CPU_NAME="ax45mpv"
    36→CPU_FLAG="-mtune=andes-45-series"
    37→APP_INCLUDE_PATH="-I./../../Examples/unit_func/include -I./../../Include -I./../../internal"
    38→LIB_INCLUDE_PATH="-I./Include -I./internal"
    39→if [[ ${ENA_VEC_ISA} -eq 1 ]]; then
    40→    if [[ ${VEC_CONFIG} -eq 0 ]]; then

    99→    # RUN_WRAPPER="riscv-qemu-wrapper-v256_d256_b256"

#!/usr/bin/env bash
################################################################################
# Input:
#  $1: extra compilation flags or macro for both libray and application
################################################################################
CWD=$(pwd)

#----- set up the tool name -----
source ../NLIB_CROSS_V5_RV64

#----- build and run switches -----
ENA_BUILD_LIBNN=1
ENA_RUN_CIFAR10=0
ENA_RUN_GRU=0
ENA_RUN_PNET=0
ENA_RUN_MTCNN=0
ENA_RUN_MTCNN_DW=0
ENA_RUN_DW_TEST=0
ENA_RUN_DW_NONSQ_TEST=0
ENA_RUN_UNIT_FUNC=1
RUN_CONFIG=2        #0: run app on board, 1: run app on SID, 2: run app on QEMU
ENA_CLANG=0         #0: use gcc as compiler, 1: use clang as compiler
ENA_RUN_TWICE=0     #0: run function once, 1: run function twice
ENA_VEC_ISA=1       #0: disable v-ext (for measuring plain C algo performance), 1: enable v-ext
ENA_VDOT=1          #0: use the default v-ext algorithm, 1: use vdot v-ext algorithm
ENA_VEC_INTRIN=0    #0: using the inline assembly version v-ext algorithm, 1: use the intrinsic version v-ext algorithm
ENA_HVM=0           #0: place the program's data on cache, 1: place the program's data on HVM
ENA_HPM=0           #0: disable the performance monitor, 1: enable the performance monitor
ENA_ACE_RVV=0       #0: disabe the ACE-RVV, 1: enable the ACE-RVV
ENA_ZVLSSEG=0       #0: disable zvlsseg extension, 1: enable zvlsseg extension
ENA_VL4=1           #0: disable VL4 extension, 1: enable VL4 extension
VEC_CONFIG=4        #0: zve32x, 1: zve32f, 2: zve64x, 3: zve64f, 4: zv64d

#----- flags of library and app -----
CPU_NAME="ax45mpv"
CPU_FLAG="-mtune=andes-45-series"
APP_INCLUDE_PATH="-I./../../Examples/unit_func/include -I./../../Include -I./../../internal"
LIB_INCLUDE_PATH="-I./Include -I./internal"
if [[ ${ENA_VEC_ISA} -eq 1 ]]; then
    if [[ ${VEC_CONFIG} -eq 0 ]]; then
        VEC_CFG="zve32x"
    elif [[ ${VEC_CONFIG} -eq 1 ]]; then
        VEC_CFG="zve32f"
    elif [[ ${VEC_CONFIG} -eq 2 ]]; then
        VEC_CFG="zve64x"
    elif [[ ${VEC_CONFIG} -eq 3 ]]; then
        VEC_CFG="zve64f"
    else
        VEC_CFG="zve64d"
    fi

    CPU_FLAG+=" -mext-vector=${VEC_CFG} -DNDS_VEC_RVV_VERSION=1000"
    # export the VEC_CFG environment variable for selecting the proper QEMU conf
    export VEC_CFG="${VEC_CFG}"
fi
if [[ ${ENA_VEC_ISA} -eq 1 ]] && [[ ${ENA_VDOT} -eq 0 ]]; then
    CPU_FLAG+=" -UENA_NDS_V5_VEC_DOT_PROD"
fi
if [[ ${ENA_VEC_ISA} -eq 1 ]] && [[ ${ENA_ZVLSSEG} -eq 1 ]]; then
    CPU_FLAG+=" -DENA_VEC_ISA_ZVLSSEG"
fi
if [[ ${ENA_VEC_ISA} -eq 1 ]] && [[ ${ENA_CLANG} -eq 1 ]] && [[ ${ENA_VEC_INTRIN} -eq 1 ]]; then
    # please note that now only clang supports RVV intrinsic
    CPU_FLAG+=" -DENA_VEC_INTRINSIC -fno-unroll-loops"
fi
COMMON_CFLAG="${CPU_FLAG} -O3 -Wall -Werror -ffunction-sections -fdata-sections -DPF_COUNTER ${1}"    #shared flags for library ad app
if [[ ${ENA_ACE_RVV} -eq 1 ]]; then
    ACE_LIB_PATH=$(readlink -f ${CWD}/../../ace_lib_exp)
    export -p ACE_LIB_PATH="${ACE_LIB_PATH}"
    COMMON_CFLAG+=" -DENA_ACE_RVV -Wa,-mace=${CWD}/../../ace_lib_exp/libacetool.so"
fi
APP_CFLAG="${COMMON_CFLAG} -Wl,-gc-sections -DENA_FS_VS ${APP_INCLUDE_PATH}"
LIB_CFLAG="${COMMON_CFLAG} -fno-strict-aliasing ${LIB_INCLUDE_PATH}"

# enable mvh
if [[ ${RUN_CONFIG} -eq 0 ]] || [[ ${RUN_CONFIG} -eq 2 ]]; then
    APP_CFLAG="${APP_CFLAG} -mvh"
fi

if [[ ${ENA_RUN_TWICE} -eq 1 ]]; then
    APP_CFLAG="${APP_CFLAG} -DENA_RUN_TWICE"
fi
if [[ ${ENA_HVM} -eq 1 ]]; then
    LIB_CFLAG+=" -mcmodel=large -DENA_HVM"
    APP_CFLAG+=" -mcmodel=large -DENA_HVM"
fi
if [[ ${ENA_HPM} -eq 1 ]]; then
    APP_CFLAG+=" -DENA_CACHE_PFM -DENA_L2C_PFM"
fi

if [[ ${ENA_VL4} -eq 1 ]]; then
    APP_CFLAG+=" -DENA_VL4"
fi

#----- set up the wrapper and compiler  -----
if [[ ${RUN_CONFIG} -eq 2 ]]; then
    # RUN_WRAPPER="riscv-qemu-wrapper-v128_d128_b128"
    # RUN_WRAPPER="riscv-qemu-wrapper-v256_d128_b128"
    # RUN_WRAPPER="riscv-qemu-wrapper-v256_d256_b256"
    # RUN_WRAPPER="riscv-qemu-wrapper-v512_d256_b256"
    RUN_WRAPPER="riscv-qemu-wrapper-v512_d512_b512"
    # RUN_WRAPPER="riscv-qemu-wrapper-v1024_d512_b512"
    # RUN_WRAPPER="riscv-qemu-wrapper-v1024_d1024_b512"
elif [[ ${RUN_CONFIG} -eq 1 ]]; then
    # RUN_WRAPPER="riscv-sim-wrapper-vec_v1_0_v128_d128_b128"
    # RUN_WRAPPER="riscv-sim-wrapper-vec_v1_0_v256_d128_b128"
    # RUN_WRAPPER="riscv-sim-wrapper-vec_v1_0_v256_d256_b256"
    # RUN_WRAPPER="riscv-sim-wrapper-vec_v1_0_v512_d256_b256"
    RUN_WRAPPER="riscv-sim-wrapper-vec_v1_0_v512_d512_b512"
    # RUN_WRAPPER="riscv-sim-wrapper-vec_v1_0_v1024_d512_b512"
    # RUN_WRAPPER="riscv-sim-wrapper-vec_v1_0_v1024_d1024_b512"
else
    RUN_WRAPPER="riscv64-sim-wrapper-on-board"
fi

if [[ ${ENA_CLANG} -eq 1 ]]; then
    CC="${CROSS_COMPILE}-clang"
else
    CC="${CROSS_COMPILE}-gcc"
fi

#----- extra flags -----
# -----
# automatically enable the zfh extension if the toolcahin supports it
# Note. We should check whether the zfh extension could be enabled with
# all compilation flags. The zfh extension may be supported with the
# toolchain; however, it's disabled by some compilation flags.
# -----
# check whether the zfh extension could be enabled
${CC} -mzfh -E -dM ${COMMON_CFLAG} - < /dev/null &> /dev/null
if [ $? -eq 0 ]; then
    # check whether the toolchain is [single|double]-abi
    # (for the spcial case of v5 toolchain + clang)
    ${CC} -mzfh -E -dM ${COMMON_CFLAG} - < /dev/null | grep '__riscv_float_abi_single\|__riscv_float_abi_double' &> /dev/null
    if [ $? -eq 0 ]; then
        APP_CFLAG="${APP_CFLAG} -mzfh"
        LIB_CFLAG="${LIB_CFLAG} -mzfh"
    fi
fi

#----- build the lib -----
if [ ${ENA_BUILD_LIBNN} -eq 1 ];then
    cd ../../
    if [ ${ENA_VEC_ISA} -eq 0 ]; then
        build.sh "NX45" "${LIB_CFLAG}" "${CC}"
    else
        build.sh "${CPU_NAME}" "${LIB_CFLAG}" "${CC}"
    fi
    build_ret=$?
    cd ${CWD}

    if [ ${build_ret} -ne 0 ]; then
        exit 1
    fi
fi



#----- app running configuration -----
# config-0: run app without fast version libnn function and with application level profiling
# config-1: run app with fast version libnn function and application level profiling
# config-2: run app with fast version libnn function and function level profiling
# config-3: run app with printing check sum and debug info
APP_RUN_CONFIG=2

if [ ${APP_RUN_CONFIG} == 0 ]; then
    CFLAGS="${APP_CFLAG}"
elif [ ${APP_RUN_CONFIG} == 1 ]; then
    CFLAGS="${APP_CFLAG} -DFAST_VERSION"
elif [ ${APP_RUN_CONFIG} == 2 ]; then
    CFLAGS="${APP_CFLAG} -DFAST_VERSION -DFUNCTION_PF_ANALYZE"
else
    CFLAGS="${APP_CFLAG} -UPF_COUNTER -DPRINT_GOLDEN -DCHECK -DFAST_VERSION"
fi


#$1: application name
function cmp_result {
    local app=${1}

    #compare the results with goldens
    cmp ${app}.log ../golden/${app}.log
    local ret=$?
    echo
    if [ ${ret} == 0 ] ; then
        echo ">>>>> accuracy checking pass. <<<<<"
    else
        echo ">>>>> accuracy checking FAIL !!! <<<<<"
    fi
    echo
}

if [ ${ENA_RUN_CIFAR10} -eq 1 ];then
    echo
    echo "------------------"
    echo "Running CIFAR10...."
    echo "------------------"
    echo
    make clean_cifar10 -f Makefile_test.mak
    make cifar10 -f Makefile_test.mak CFLAGS="${CFLAGS} -fno-strict-aliasing"
    ${RUN_WRAPPER} cifar10.adx

    if [ ${APP_RUN_CONFIG} == 3 ]; then
        cmp_result "cifar10"
    fi
fi

if [ ${ENA_RUN_GRU} -eq 1 ];then
    echo
    echo "------------------"
    echo "Running GRU...."
    echo "------------------"
    echo
    make clean_gru -f Makefile_test.mak
    make gru -f Makefile_test.mak CFLAGS="${CFLAGS} -fno-strict-aliasing"
    ${RUN_WRAPPER} gru.adx

    if [ ${APP_RUN_CONFIG} == 3 ]; then
        cmp_result "gru"
    fi
fi

if [ ${ENA_RUN_PNET} -eq 1 ];then
    echo
    echo "------------------"
    echo "Running PNET...."

    echo "------------------"
    echo
    cd ../../Examples/unit_func/
    ./run.sh "${CC}" "${CWD}/${RUN_WRAPPER}" "${APP_CFLAG}"
fi

        echo ${coverage_status} >> ${COV_CSV}
    done

#######################################################
# collect the code size of each section
#   $1: test function name
#######################################################
calculate_code_size () {
    local log_file build_cmd tmp_size_log

    echo -n "${1}," >> ${SIZE_CSV}
    log_file="${LOG_DIR}/${1}_empty.log"
    build_cmd="${CC} ${DEFAULT_APP_CFLAGS} -O3 ${EXTRA_FLAG} -DENA_DUMP_GOLDEN=${ENA_DUMP_GOLDEN} -DENA_MEASURE_PERF=${ENA_MEASURE_PERF} -DGETENV_TESTDIR=${UNIT_TEST_PATH} ${INC_DIR} -o ${ADX_DIR}/t_$1_empty.adx ${UNIT_TEST_PATH}/t_$1.c ${EMPTY_LIB} -lm"
    #use the linker specified by the user
    if [[ -n ${LD} ]]; then
        build_cmd="${build_cmd} -fuse-ld=${LD}"
    fi

    # build the executable
    ${build_cmd} 2>>${log_file} | tee -a ${log_file}

    # get the size info
    tmp_size_log="${LOG_DIR}/size.log"
    ${SIZE} ${ADX_DIR}/t_${1}.adx       | tail -1 > ${tmp_size_log}
    ${SIZE} ${ADX_DIR}/t_${1}_empty.adx | tail -1 >> ${tmp_size_log}

    # calculate the size of each section
    awk '
        NR==1 {split($0,non_empty)}
        NR==2 {split($0,empty)}
        END {for(i=1;i<=5;i++) printf "%u,", non_empty[i] - empty[i]}
    ' ${tmp_size_log} >> ${SIZE_CSV}
    echo >> ${SIZE_CSV}  #new line
}

#######################################################
# run the unit test and check the accuracy results
#   $1: test function name
#   $2: test function group name (for grouping excel results)
#######################################################
function run_and_check {
    # skip the fp16 functions when the zfh extension is not enabled
    if [ ${ENA_FP16} -eq 0 ] && [[ "${1}" == *"f16"* ]]; then
        accuracy_unsupport_num=$((accuracy_unsupport_num + 1))
        echo "$2,$1,x,x," >> ${PERF_CSV}
        return
    fi

    # skip the integer 4-bit functions when the v-ext enabled but the VLN8 not enabled
    if [[ ${EXTRA_FLAG} =~ -mext-vector ]] && [[ ${ENA_VL4} -eq 0 ]] && [[ ${1} =~ s4 ]]; then
        accuracy_unsupport_num=$((accuracy_unsupport_num + 1))
        echo "$2,$1,x,x," >> ${PERF_CSV}
        return
    fi

    log "--- $1 ---"
    local log_file="${LOG_DIR}/${1}.log"
    build_cmd="${CC} ${DEFAULT_APP_CFLAGS} -O3 -DENA_DUMP_GOLDEN=${ENA_DUMP_GOLDEN} -DENA_MEASURE_PERF=${ENA_MEASURE_PERF} -DGETENV_TESTDIR=${UNIT_TEST_PATH} ${INC_DIR} -o ${ADX_DIR}/t_$1.adx ${UNIT_TEST_PATH}/t_$1.c ${LINKED_LIB} ${EXTRA_FLAG} -lm"
    #use the linker specified by the user
    if [[ -n ${LD} ]]; then
        build_cmd="${build_cmd} -fuse-ld=${LD}"
    fi

    echo ${build_cmd} > ${log_file}
    ${build_cmd} 2>>${log_file} | tee -a ${log_file}
    if [ ${PIPESTATUS[0]} == 0 ]; then  #check the compiling successful or not
        # dump the disassembly
        ${OBJDUMP} "${ADX_DIR}/t_${1}.adx" -S > "${OBJDUMP_DIR}/t_${1}.objdump"

        log "Start at $(date)"
        ${SIM_WRAPPER} ${ADX_DIR}/t_$1.adx >> ${log_file} 2>&1
        ret=$?
        log "End at $(date)"

        # check correctness
        if [ ${ret} == 0 ]; then
            # check whehter the program is executed (For the case of AndeSim
            # doesn't launch successfully. BTW, AndeSim doesn't return error
            # code even if it enounters some issues.)
            # Here we use the ALWAYS present string as the criteria whether
            # the test program is executed.
            grep -w 'pass\|fail' ${log_file} &> /dev/null
            if [ $? -ne 0 ]; then
                accuracy_fail_list+=("${1}")
                echo "Warning! There is no 'pass' or 'fail' string is parsed in this log file; therefore, the program may not executed." >> ${log_file}
            else
                accuracy_pass_num=$((accuracy_pass_num + 1))
            fi

            if [ ${ENA_GREP_PF_TO_CSV} == 1 ]; then
                #dump inst/cycle into csv
                echo -e "$2,$1,\c" >> ${PERF_CSV}
                grep -w 'inst' ${log_file}  | awk '{printf "%ld,", $5}' >> ${PERF_CSV}
                grep -w 'cycle' ${log_file} | awk '{printf "%ld,", $5}' >> ${PERF_CSV}
                echo >> ${PERF_CSV}  #new line
            fi

            if [ ${ENA_GREP_PARAM_TO_CSV} == 1 ]; then
                # following parser will work properly only as below assumptions
                # are valid
                #   (1) each parameter is seperated with semicolon except the
                #       last one
                #   (2) the parameters are surrounded by the "param_hint_begin"
                #       and "param_hint_end"

                #dump the parameters into csv
                local param_hint_begin="___PARAMETERS_BEGIN___"
                local param_hint_end="___PARAMETERS_END___"
                local tmp_param_str=$(sed -n "/${param_hint_begin}/,/${param_hint_end}/p" ${log_file})

                # remove all newlines
                tmp_param_str=${tmp_param_str//$'\r'/}
                tmp_param_str=${tmp_param_str//$'\n'/}
                # remove hint string
                tmp_param_str=${tmp_param_str//${param_hint_begin}/}
                tmp_param_str=${tmp_param_str//${param_hint_end}/,}
                # make each parameter being in one line
                tmp_param_str=${tmp_param_str//;/;$'\n'}
                echo -e "$2,$1,${tmp_param_str}" >> ${PARAM_CSV}
            fi
        else
            accuracy_fail_list+=("${1}")
        fi
    else
        # build the executable fail
        accuracy_fail_list+=("${1}")
    fi

    if [[ ${ENA_MEASURE_CODE_SIZE} -eq 1 ]]; then
        calculate_code_size "${1}"
    fi
}

###############################################################################
# build and run the target configuration diagnosis program
#  $1: source file name of the program
###############################################################################
function target_cfg_diagnosis {
    local src_fname="target_cfg_diagnosis"
    log "--- ${src_fname} ---"
    local log_file="${LOG_DIR}/${src_fname}.log"
    build_cmd="${CC} ${DEFAULT_APP_CFLAGS} -O3 -o ${ADX_DIR}/$src_fname.adx ${UNIT_TEST_PATH}/$src_fname.c ${EXTRA_FLAG}"
    echo ${build_cmd} > ${log_file}

    ${build_cmd} 2>>${log_file} | tee -a ${log_file}
    if [ ${PIPESTATUS[0]} == 0 ]; then  #check the compiling successful or not
        log "Start at $(date)"
        ${SIM_WRAPPER} ${ADX_DIR}/${src_fname}.adx >> ${log_file} 2>&1
        ret=$?
        log "End at $(date)"
    fi
}

#######################################################
# build the executables
#   $1: test function name
#######################################################
function build_executable {
    log "--- $1 ---"
    local log_file="${LOG_DIR}/${1}.log"
    build_cmd="${CC} ${DEFAULT_APP_CFLAGS} -O3 -DENA_DUMP_GOLDEN=${ENA_DUMP_GOLDEN} -DENA_MEASURE_PERF=${ENA_MEASURE_PERF} ${INC_DIR} -o ${ADX_DIR}/t_$1.adx ${UNIT_TEST_PATH}/t_$1.c ${LINKED_LIB} ${EXTRA_FLAG} -lm"

    #use the linker specified by the user
    if [[ -n ${LD} ]]; then
        build_cmd+=" -fuse-ld=${LD}"
    fi

    echo ${build_cmd} > ${log_file}
    ${build_cmd} 2>>${log_file} | tee -a ${log_file}
    if [[ ${PIPESTATUS[0]} != 0 ]]; then  #check the compiling successful or not
        # build the executable fail
        accuracy_fail_list+=("${1}")
    fi
}

#######################################################
# Some function's algorithm implementaions are hardware
# configuration dependant; thus, there are mutiple
# algoirhtms for different hardware configurations and
# the code coverage is impossible to be 100% as executing
# the function under one hardware configuration. Here we
# execute these functions with all hardware configuration
# simulator warpper to make the coverage reach 100%.
#######################################################
function run_special_case_for_rvv () {
    local sim_wrapper_list=($(ls ${SIM_WRAPPER%v*}*))
    local log_file="${LOG_DIR}/special_case.log"

    for sim_wrapper in "${sim_wrapper_list[@]}"; do
      for fun in "${SPECIAL_CASE_RVV[@]}"; do
          echo "${sim_wrapper} ${ADX_DIR}/t_${fun}.adx" >> ${log_file}
          ${sim_wrapper} ${ADX_DIR}/t_${fun}.adx >> ${log_file}

file.content:
# check whether the compiler name is valid
if [[ ${CPU_NAME} =~ ^(n225|d23|[nad]25|a27|[nad]45)$ ]] ||
   [[ ${CPU_NAME} =~ ^a46mp(v(_purec|_seg)?|)$ ]]; then
    if [[ ! ${COMPILER_NAME} =~ ^riscv32-elf-(gcc|clang)$ ]]; then
        echo -e "Error! Pleas set the compiler to an appropriate one.\n" \
                "The available compilers are:\n" \
                "    riscv32-elf-gcc\n" \
                "    riscv32-elf-clang"
        touch "../${ERROR_LOG}"
        exit 1
    fi
elif [[ ${CPU_NAME} =~ ^[na]x(25|45)$ ]] ||
     [[ ${CPU_NAME} =~ ^nx27v(_elen64(_felen64)?|_purec)?$ ]] ||
     [[ ${CPU_NAME} =~ ^ax(45|46)mpv(_elen64(_felen64)?(_seg)?|_seg|_purec)?$ ]] ||
     [[ ${CPU_NAME} == ax46mp ]] ||
     [[ ${CPU_NAME} =~ ^ax(65|66)$ ]]; then
    if [[ ! ${COMPILER_NAME} =~ ^riscv64-elf-(gcc|clang)$ ]]; then
        echo -e "Error! Pleas set the compiler to an appropriate one.\n" \
                "The available compilers are:\n" \
                "    riscv64-elf-gcc\n" \
                "    riscv64-elf-clang"
        touch "../${ERROR_LOG}"
        exit 1
    fi
else
    # here will not be reached
    echo "Error! ${1} is not a supported CPU !"
fi

# use the customized linker script for moving the data into local memory
if [[ ${CPU_NAME} =~ ^(n225|d23|n25|a25|d25|nx25|ax25|a27|n45|a45|d45|nx45|ax45|a46mp|ax46mp)$ ]]; then
    CPU_FLAGS+=" -DENA_LM"
fi

# select the library to be linkd
if [[ ${CPU_NAME} =~ ^(nx27v|ax45mpv|a46mpv|ax46mpv|ax66)$ ]]; then
    CPU_FLAGS+=" -lnn_v"
elif [[ ${CPU_NAME} =~ ^(nx27v|ax45mpv|ax46mpv|ax66)_elen64$ ]]; then
    CPU_FLAGS+=" -lnn_v_elen64"
elif [[ ${CPU_NAME} =~ ^(nx27v|ax45mpv|ax46mpv|ax66)_elen64_felen64$ ]]; then
    CPU_FLAGS+=" -lnn_v_elen64_felen64"
elif [[ ${CPU_NAME} =~ ^(ax45mpv|a46mpv|ax46mpv|ax66)_seg$ ]]; then
    CPU_FLAGS+=" -lnn_v_seg"
elif [[ ${CPU_NAME} =~ ^(ax45mpv|ax46mpv|ax66)_elen64_seg$ ]]; then
    CPU_FLAGS+=" -lnn_v_elen64_seg"
elif [[ ${CPU_NAME} =~ ^(ax45mpv|ax46mpv|ax66)_elen64_felen64_seg$ ]]; then
    CPU_FLAGS+=" -lnn_v_elen64_felen64_seg"
else
    CPU_FLAGS+=" -lnn"
fi

# select the running on board wrapper
if [[ ${CPU_NAME} =~ ^ax45mpv(_elen64(_felen64)?(_seg)?|_seg|_purec)?$ ]]; then
   SIM_WRAPPER=$(readlink -f ${LIB_ROOT}/project/ax45mpv/riscv64-sim-wrapper-on-board)
elif [[ ${CPU_NAME} =~ ^nx27v(_purec|_elen64|_elen64_felen64)?$ ]]; then
   SIM_WRAPPER=$(readlink -f ${LIB_ROOT}/project/nx27v/riscv64-sim-wrapper-on-board)
elif [[ ${CPU_NAME} =~ ^(n|a)x(25|45)$ ]] ||
     [[ ${CPU_NAME} == ax46mp ]]; then
   SIM_WRAPPER=$(readlink -f ${LIB_ROOT}/project/ax25/riscv64-sim-wrapper-on-board)
elif [[ ${CPU_NAME} =~ ^a46mpv(_seg|_purec)?$  ]]; then
   SIM_WRAPPER=$(readlink -f ${LIB_ROOT}/project/a46mpv/riscv32-sim-wrapper-on-board)
elif [[ ${CPU_NAME} =~ ^ax46mpv(_elen64(_felen64)?(_seg)?|_seg|_purec)?$ ]]; then
   SIM_WRAPPER=$(readlink -f ${LIB_ROOT}/project/ax46mpv/riscv64-sim-wrapper-on-board)
elif [[ ${CPU_NAME} == ax65 ]]; then
   SIM_WRAPPER=$(readlink -f ${LIB_ROOT}/project/ax65/riscv64-sim-wrapper-on-board)
elif [[ ${CPU_NAME} =~ ^ax66(_elen64(_felen64)?(_seg)?|_seg|_purec)?$ ]]; then
   SIM_WRAPPER=$(readlink -f ${LIB_ROOT}/project/ax66/riscv64-sim-wrapper-on-board)
else
   SIM_WRAPPER=$(readlink -f ${LIB_ROOT}/project/d25/riscv32-sim-wrapper-on-board)
fi

# -----
# Automatically enable the zfh extension if the toolcahin supports it.
# Note-1. We should check whether the zfh extension could be enabled with
# all compilation flags. The zfh extension may be supported by the
# toolchain; however, it's disabled by some compilation flags.
# Note-2. Now the zfh extension is supported only on NX27V and 45-series
# cores.
# -----
# check whether the zfh extension could be enabled
${COMPILER_NAME} -mzfh -E -dM ${CPU_FLAGS} - < /dev/null &> /dev/null
if [ $? -eq 0 ]; then
    # check whether the toolchain is [single|double]-abi
    # (for the spcial case of v5 toolchain + clang)
    ${COMPILER_NAME} -mzfh -E -dM ${CPU_FLAGS} - < /dev/null | grep '__riscv_float_abi_single\|__riscv_float_abi_double' &> /dev/null
    if [ $? -eq 0 ]; then
        # check whether the core is NX27V/45-series/46-series/60-series
        if [[ ${CPU_NAME} =~ (nx27v|45|46|65|66) ]]; then
            CPU_FLAGS="${CPU_FLAGS} -mzfh"
        fi
    fi
fi

##### main ######
rm -f ${TEST_LOG}
log "***************************************************************************************"
log ${LIB_NAME} performance testing begin ...
log "Input 1(CPU): ${CPU_NAME}"
log "Input 2(COMPILER): ${COMPILER_NAME}"
log "Input 3(TEST_TARGET): ${TARGET}"
log "SIM_WRAPPER: ${SIM_WRAPPER}"
log "EXTRA_CFLAGS: ${CPU_FLAGS}"
log "***************************************************************************************"

EXTRA_CFLAGS="${CPU_FLAGS}"
${LIB_ROOT}/test.sh "" "${COMPILER_NAME}" "${SIM_WRAPPER}" "${TARGET}" "BS3" "${EXTRA_CFLAGS} -DENA_TEST_PERF -DENA_DUMP_PARAMETERS"

log "***************************************************************************************"
log ${LIB_NAME} performance testing end.
log "***************************************************************************************"

# move the log int test_sh_<TIMESTAMP> folder
out_folder_list=($(find . -name "test_sh*" -type d | sort))
mv "test_perf.log" -t "${out_folder_list[${#arr[@]}-1]}"

file.content:
#!/bin/bash

#==================== controlling switches (begin) ====================
RUN_MODE=0          # the mode used to run QEMU
                    #  - 0: standalone mode
                    #  - 1: GDB server mode
#==================== controlling switches (end)   ====================

OLD_PWD=`pwd`
ADX_PATH="$( cd "$( dirname "$1" )" && echo `pwd`/$(basename "$1"))"
THIS_DIR="$( cd "$( dirname "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}" )" && pwd )"

# this value should be replaced by build system when start testing
SIMULATOR_DIR=${THIS_DIR}

# QEMU configs
QEMU=${QEMU_SYS_RV64}
CPU=andes-ax45mpv,vext_spec=v1.0,vlen=1024
if [[ -z ${VEC_CFG} ]]; then
    # by default, select the zve64d config
    CONFIG=${SIMULATOR_DIR}/qemu_cfg/zve64d/ADP-AE350-AX45MPV-1C_dw0_mw1.cfg
elif [[ ! ${VEC_CFG} =~ ^zve32(x|f)$ ]] && [[ ! ${VEC_CFG} =~ ^zve64(x|f|d)$ ]]; then
    # for the case of using unsupported VEC_CFG
    echo "Warning! The VEC_CFF=${VEC_CFG} is not a supported vector configuration. Select the default vector configuration (zve64d) for QEMU."
    CONFIG=${SIMULATOR_DIR}/qemu_cfg/zve64d/ADP-AE350-AX45MPV-1C_dw0_mw1.cfg
else
    CONFIG=${SIMULATOR_DIR}/qemu_cfg/${VEC_CFG}/ADP-AE350-AX45MPV-1C_dw0_mw1.cfg
fi

# check if the execution file is available
if [ "$1" = "" ] || [ ! -f "$1" ]; then
    echo ""
    echo "No such file $1"
    echo ""
    exit 1
fi

# get the executable file
RUN_FILE=$ADX_PATH

# Run qemu
EXTRA_QEMU_CONF=""
if [[ ${RUN_MODE} -eq 1 ]]; then
    EXTRA_QEMU_CONF="-S -s"
fi
$QEMU -readconfig $CONFIG -M andes_ae350 -cpu $CPU -nographic -semihosting -bios $RUN_FILE ${EXTRA_QEMU_CONF} 2>&1

ret=$?
cd ${OLD_PWD}
exit ${ret}

content.text:
Here's everything you need for AX45MPV with VLEN=1024:

## 1. Build the Library

```bash
# Set toolchain in PATH
export PATH=/local/nick/SW_Release_cp/ast530/nds64le-elf-newlib-v5d/bin:$PATH

# Build
cd /local/nick/libnn
./build.sh AX45MPV "" riscv64-elf-gcc
```

This auto-selects flags: `-mtune=andes-45-series -mext-vector -DENA_VEC_ISA -DMAX_VLEN=1024 -DNDS_VEC_RVV_VERSION=1000 -DENA_NDS_V5_VEC_DOT_PROD` plus the standard `-Wall -Werror -O3`. Output: `libnn.a` in repo root.

## 2. Run Accuracy Tests (via test.sh)

```bash
export PATH=/local/nick/SW_Release_cp/ast530/nds64le-elf-newlib-v5d/bin:$PATH
export QEMU_SYS_RV64=/local/nick/qemu_andes/build/qemu-system-riscv64

mkdir -p /local/nick/libnn/build_ax45mpv && cd /local/nick/libnn/build_ax45mpv

# QEMU-based accuracy test (BS3 mode — uses pre-built multilib)
../test.sh "" riscv64-elf-gcc \
    ../project/ax45mpv/riscv-qemu-wrapper-v1024_d1024_b512 \
    QEMU BS3 \
    "-mtune=andes-45-series -mext-vector -DNDS_VEC_RVV_VERSION=1000"

# DAILY mode (rebuilds library + accuracy + code coverage)
../test.sh "" riscv64-elf-gcc \
    ../project/ax45mpv/riscv-qemu-wrapper-v1024_d1024_b512 \
    QEMU DAILY \
    "-mtune=andes-45-series -mext-vector -DNDS_VEC_RVV_VERSION=1000"
```

The QEMU wrapper (`riscv-qemu-wrapper-v1024_d1024_b512`) launches QEMU with:
- CPU: `andes-ax45mpv,vext_spec=v1.0,vlen=1024`
- Machine: `andes_ae350`
- Config: `project/ax45mpv/qemu_cfg/zve64d/ADP-AE350-AX45MPV-1C_dw0_mw1.cfg`
- Semihosting enabled

**Sandbox note**: In Claude Code's sandbox, semihosting output goes to fd 9 instead of stdout. If you run QEMU manually, redirect with `9>&1` or check fd 9.

## 3. Run on Board (FPGA)

```bash
# Board at sw-boards.andestech.com:1116
# Use the board wrapper — test_perf.sh selects it automatically
cd /local/nick/libnn/build_ax45mpv

../test.sh "" riscv64-elf-gcc \
    ../project/ax45mpv/riscv64-sim-wrapper-on-board \
    BOARD BS3 \
    "-mtune=andes-45-series -mext-vector -DNDS_VEC_RVV_VERSION=1000"
```

## 4. Profile Performance (test_perf.sh)

```bash
export PATH=/local/nick/SW_Release_cp/ast530/nds64le-elf-newlib-v5d/bin:$PATH

cd /local/nick/libnn
./test_perf.sh "ax45mpv" "riscv64-elf-gcc" "BOARD"
```

This automatically:
- Sets flags: `-mtune=andes-45-series -mext-vector -DNDS_VEC_RVV_VERSION=1000 -DENA_RUN_TWICE -DENA_CACHEABLE_ENV_SETUP`
- Links `-lnn_v` (vector-optimized library)
- Uses the board wrapper at `project/ax45mpv/riscv64-sim-wrapper-on-board`
- Runs all unit tests with `-DENA_TEST_PERF -DENA_DUMP_PARAMETERS`
- Outputs cycle/instruction counts to CSV

**For QEMU-based profiling** instead of board, use the `project/ax45mpv/run.sh` script (edit `RUN_CONFIG=2` for QEMU mode, already default). It uses `APP_RUN_CONFIG=2` which enables `-DFAST_VERSION -DFUNCTION_PF_ANALYZE` for function-level profiling. You'll need to set:
```bash
export QEMU_SYS_RV64=/local/nick/qemu_andes/build/qemu-system-riscv64
cd /local/nick/libnn/project/ax45mpv && ./run.sh
```

## Quick Reference

| Task | Command |
|------|---------|
| Build lib | `./build.sh AX45MPV` |
| Accuracy (QEMU) | `test.sh` with `riscv-qemu-wrapper-v1024_d1024_b512`, target=QEMU |
| Accuracy (Board) | `test.sh` with board wrapper, target=BOARD |
| Profile (Board) | `./test_perf.sh ax45mpv riscv64-elf-gcc BOARD` |
| Profile (QEMU) | `project/ax45mpv/run.sh` with `RUN_CONFIG=2` |

message.content: so vlen doesn't matter in build?

content.text:
Correct. VLEN doesn't affect the **compiled library code** — the RVV instructions are vector-length-agnostic (VLA). The code uses `vsetvl` at runtime to query the hardware's VLEN dynamically.

The `-DMAX_VLEN=1024` define in `build.sh` is used only for **compile-time buffer sizing** (e.g., stack arrays sized to the max possible vector register), not to hardcode vector lengths into the algorithms.

VLEN matters at **run time** — that's why the QEMU wrappers encode it in their names (`v1024_d1024_b512`) and pass `vlen=1024` to the QEMU CPU model:

```
CPU=andes-ax45mpv,vext_spec=v1.0,vlen=1024
```

So the same `libnn.a` binary works on hardware/simulators with any VLEN — you just pick the right wrapper/simulator config to match your target.

input.content:
# libnn — Andes Neural Network Library

Optimized neural network inference library for Andes RISC-V processors. Provides quantized (s8/s16/s4) and floating-point (f16/f32) implementations of common NN operations with multiple optimization tiers: plain C, DSP extensions, packed extensions (RV32P/RV64P), and RISC-V Vector extension (RVV 0.8/1.0).

## Build

Build the static library (`libnn.a`) for a target CPU:

```bash
./build.sh <CPU> ["<extra_flags>"] [<compiler>] [<output_path>]
```

### Arguments

| Arg | Description | Default |
|-----|-------------|---------|
| `$1` | CPU name (case insensitive) | *(required)* |
| `$2` | Extra compilation flags appended to defaults | `""` |
| `$3` | Compiler (with prefix) | Auto-selected by CPU |
| `$4` | Output path for library and objects | Repository root |

### Supported CPUs

| Family | CPUs |
|--------|------|
| NDS32 | N10, D10, D15 |
| RV32 | N225, D23, N25, D25, A25, A27, N45, D45, A45, A46MP |
| RV64 | NX25, AX25, AX27, NX45, AX45, AX46MP, AX65 |
| RV64 + Vector | NX27V, AX45MPV, AX46MPV |

### Toolchain Selection

The toolchain prefix is auto-selected based on CPU:

- **NDS32** (N10/D10/D15): `nds32le-elf-`
- **RV32**: `riscv32-elf-`
- **RV64**: `riscv64-elf-`
- **x86/x64**: no prefix (host GCC)

Override with `$3` if needed (e.g., `riscv64-elf-clang`).

### Examples

```bash
# Basic build for D25
./build.sh D25

# Build for AX45MPV with RVV (VLEN-agnostic)
export PATH=/path/to/riscv64-toolchain/bin:$PATH
./build.sh AX45MPV

# Build with extra flags
./build.sh D10 "-funroll-loops --param max-unroll-times=2"

# Build with a specific compiler
./build.sh AX45MPV "" riscv64-elf-gcc

# Build to a custom output directory
./build.sh AX45MPV "" "" /path/to/output
```

### Default Compilation Flags

All builds use: `-Wall -Werror -O3 -ffunction-sections -fdata-sections`

Additional flags are added per CPU. For example, AX45MPV adds:
`-mtune=andes-45-series -mext-vector -DENA_VEC_ISA -DMAX_VLEN=1024 -DNDS_VEC_RVV_VERSION=1000 -DENA_NDS_V5_VEC_DOT_PROD`

### Output

- `libnn.a` — the static library
- `libnn.objdump` — disassembly of the library
- `lib_objs/` — individual object files

### Note on VLEN

The library is compiled with VLEN-agnostic RVV code. The `-DMAX_VLEN=1024` define is only used for compile-time buffer sizing. The same binary runs on any VLEN — the actual vector length is determined at runtime via `vsetvl`. VLEN configuration matters only when selecting a simulator/QEMU wrapper for testing.

1→#!/usr/bin/env bash
     2→###########################################################################
     3→# This script is used to collect the libnn functions' performance with the
     4→# built-in libraries.
     5→#
     6→# Input:
     7→#  $1: CPU name
     8→#      supported CPU list:
     9→#      (1) CPU w/o VPU:      n225, d23, n25, a25, d25, nx25, ax25, a27, n45,
    10→#                            a45, d45, nx45, ax45, a46mp, ax46mp, ax65
    11→#      (2) 27-series w/ VPU: nx27v, nx27v_elen64, nx27v_elen64_felen64,
    12→#                            nx27v_purec
    13→#      (3) 45-series w/ VPU: ax45mpv, ax45mpv_elen64, ax45mpv_elen64_felen64,
    14→#                            ax45mpv_seg, ax45mpv_elen64_seg,
    15→#                            ax45mpv_elen64_felen64_seg, ax45mpv_purec
    16→#      (4) 46-series w/ VPU: a46mpv, a46mpv_seg, a46mpv_purec, ax46mpv,
    17→#                            ax46mpv_elen64, ax46mpv_elen64_felen64,
    18→#                            ax46mpv_seg, ax46mpv_elen64_seg,
    19→#                            ax46mpv_elen64_felen64_seg, ax46mpv_purec
    20→#      (5) 60-series w/ VPU: ax66, ax66_elen64, ax66_elen64_felen64, ax66_seg,
    21→#                            ax66_elen64_seg, ax66_elen64_felen64_seg,
    22→#                            ax66_purec
    23→#  $2: compiler name
    24→#      Now only the elf toolchains are supported and the supported compilers
    25→#      are:
    26→#        - riscv32-elf-gcc
    27→#        - riscv32-elf-clang
    28→#        - riscv64-elf-gcc
    29→#        - riscv64-elf-clang
    30→#  $3: the target to run the executables
    31→#      Now the target could only be BOARD.
    32→#
    33→# Example:
    34→#  ex-1: measure the performance on FPGA with D25 bitmap and clang compiler
    35→#  ./test_perf.sh "d25" "riscv32-elf-gcc" "BOARD"
    36→#
    37→#  ex-2: measure the performance on FPGA with AX25 bitmap and clang compiler
    38→#  ./test_perf.sh "ax25" "riscv64-elf-clang" "BOARD"
    39→#
    40→#  ex-3: measure the performance on FPGA with NX27V bitmap and gcc compiler
    41→#  ./test_perf.sh "nx27v" "riscv64-elf-gcc" "BOARD"
    42→#
    43→#  ex-4: measure the pure C algorithm performance on FPGA with NX27V bitmap
    44→#  and clang compiler
    45→#  ./test_perf.sh "nx27v_purec" "riscv64-elf-clang" "BOARD"
    46→###########################################################################
    47→
    48→CPU_NAME=${1,,}   ## always convert to lower case
    49→COMPILER_NAME="$2"
    50→if [ -z ${3} ]; then
    51→    TARGET="BOARD"
    52→else
    53→    TARGET="${3}"
    54→fi
    55→
    56→function log () {
    57→    echo "$*" | tee -a ${TEST_LOG}
    58→}
    59→
    60→##### misc settings #####
    61→LIB_NAME="libnn"
    62→LIB_ROOT=$(readlink -f `dirname ${0}`)
    63→TEST_LOG="test_perf.log"
    64→ERROR_LOG="diff_log"
    65→
    66→# set the proper cflag accrording to the CPU_NAME
    67→if [[ ${CPU_NAME} == n225 ]]; then
    68→    CPU_FLAGS="-mext-zc"
    69→elif [[ ${CPU_NAME} == d23 ]]; then
    70→    CPU_FLAGS="-mext-dsp -mext-zc"
    71→elif [[ ${CPU_NAME} =~ ^n(x)?25$ ]]; then
    72→    CPU_FLAGS=""
    73→elif [[ ${CPU_NAME} =~ ^(a|d|ax)25$ ]] ||
    74→     [[ ${CPU_NAME} =~ ^a27$ ]]; then
    75→    CPU_FLAGS="-mext-dsp"
    76→elif [[ ${CPU_NAME} =~ ^n(x)?45$ ]]; then
    77→    CPU_FLAGS="-mtune=andes-45-series"
    78→elif [[ ${CPU_NAME} =~ ^(a|d|ax)45$ ]]; then
    79→    CPU_FLAGS="-mtune=andes-45-series -mext-dsp"
    80→elif [[ ${CPU_NAME} =~ ^(a|ax)46mp$ ]]; then
    81→    CPU_FLAGS="-mtune=andes-45-series -mext-dsp -mext-zc"
    82→elif [[ ${CPU_NAME} == ax65 ]]; then
    83→    CPU_FLAGS="-mtune=andes-60-series -DENA_RUN_TWICE -DENA_CACHEABLE_ENV_SETUP"
    84→elif [[ ${CPU_NAME} =~ ^nx27v(_elen64|_elen64_felen64)?$ ]]; then
    85→    CPU_FLAGS="-mext-vector -DNDS_VEC_RVV_VERSION=1000 -DENA_RUN_TWICE -DENA_CACHEABLE_ENV_SETUP"
    86→elif [[ ${CPU_NAME} =~ ^ax45mpv(_elen64(_felen64)?(_seg)?|_seg)?$ ]]; then
    87→    CPU_FLAGS="-mtune=andes-45-series -mext-vector -DNDS_VEC_RVV_VERSION=1000 -DENA_RUN_TWICE -DENA_CACHEABLE_ENV_SETUP"
    88→elif [[ ${CPU_NAME} =~ ^a46mpv(_seg)?$ ]]; then
    89→    ${COMPILER_NAME} -E -dM - < /dev/null | grep -qw "__riscv_float_abi_soft"
    90→    is_soft_ft_abi=$?
    91→    if [[ ${is_soft_ft_abi} -eq 0 ]]; then
    92→        VEC_CFG="zve32x"
    93→    else
    94→        VEC_CFG="zve32f"
    95→    fi
    96→
    97→    CPU_FLAGS="-mtune=andes-45-series -mext-vector=${VEC_CFG} -mext-zc -DNDS_VEC_RVV_VERSION=1000 -DENA_RUN_TWICE -DENA_CACHEABLE_ENV_SETUP"
    98→elif [[ ${CPU_NAME} =~ ^ax46mpv(_elen64(_felen64)?(_seg)?|_seg?)?$ ]]; then
    99→    CPU_FLAGS="-mtune=andes-45-series -mext-vector -mext-zc -DNDS_VEC_RVV_VERSION=1000 -DENA_RUN_TWICE -DENA_CACHEABLE_ENV_SETUP"
   100→elif [[ ${CPU_NAME} =~ ^ax66(_elen64(_felen64)?(_seg)?|_seg)?$ ]]; then
   101→    CPU_FLAGS="-mtune=andes-60-series -mext-vector -DNDS_VEC_RVV_VERSION=1000 -DENA_RUN_TWICE -DENA_CACHEABLE_ENV_SETUP"
   102→elif [[ ${CPU_NAME} =~ ^(nx27v|ax45mpv)_purec$ ]]; then
   103→    CPU_FLAGS="-DENA_RUN_TWICE -DENA_CACHEABLE_ENV_SETUP"
   104→elif [[ ${CPU_NAME} =~ ^(a|ax)46mpv_purec$ ]]; then
   105→    CPU_FLAGS="-DENA_RUN_TWICE -DENA_CACHEABLE_ENV_SETUP -mext-zc"
   106→elif [[ ${CPU_NAME} == ax66_purec ]]; then
   107→    CPU_FLAGS="-mtune=andes-60-series -DENA_RUN_TWICE -DENA_CACHEABLE_ENV_SETUP"
   108→else
   109→    echo "Error! ${1} is not a supported CPU !"
   110→    echo "Supported CPUs are listed below:
   111→- CPU w/o VPU: n225, d23, n25, a25, d25, nx25, ax25, a27, n45, a45, d45, nx45, ax45, a46mp, ax46mp, ax65
   112→- 27-series w/ VPU: nx27v, nx27v_elen64, nx27v_elen64_felen64, nx27v_purec
   113→- 45-series w/ VPU: ax45mpv, ax45mpv_elen64, ax45mpv_elen64_felen64, ax45mpv_seg, ax45mpv_elen64_seg, ax45mpv_elen64_felen64_seg, ax45mpv_purec
   114→- 46-series w/ VPU: a46mpv, a46mpv_seg, a46mpv_purec, ax46mpv, ax46mpv_elen64, ax46mpv_elen64_felen64, ax46mpv_seg, ax46mpv_elen64_seg, ax46mpv_elen64_felen64_seg, ax46mpv_purec
   115→- 60-series w/ VPU: ax66, ax66_elen64, ax66_elen64_felen64, ax66_seg, ax66_elen64_seg, ax66_elen64_felen64_seg, ax66_purec"
   116→
   117→    touch "../${ERROR_LOG}"
   118→    exit 1
   119→fi
   120→
   121→# check whether the compiler name is valid
   122→if [[ ${CPU_NAME} =~ ^(n225|d23|[nad]25|a27|[nad]45)$ ]] ||
   123→   [[ ${CPU_NAME} =~ ^a46mp(v(_purec|_seg)?|)$ ]]; then
   124→    if [[ ! ${COMPILER_NAME} =~ ^riscv32-elf-(gcc|clang)$ ]]; then
   125→        echo -e "Error! Pleas set the compiler to an appropriate one.\n" \
   126→                "The available compilers are:\n" \
   127→                "    riscv32-elf-gcc\n" \
   128→                "    riscv32-elf-clang"
   129→        touch "../${ERROR_LOG}"
   130→        exit 1
   131→    fi
   132→elif [[ ${CPU_NAME} =~ ^[na]x(25|45)$ ]] ||
   133→     [[ ${CPU_NAME} =~ ^nx27v(_elen64(_felen64)?|_purec)?$ ]] ||
   134→     [[ ${CPU_NAME} =~ ^ax(45|46)mpv(_elen64(_felen64)?(_seg)?|_seg|_purec)?$ ]] ||
   135→     [[ ${CPU_NAME} == ax46mp ]] ||
   136→     [[ ${CPU_NAME} =~ ^ax(65|66)$ ]]; then
   137→    if [[ ! ${COMPILER_NAME} =~ ^riscv64-elf-(gcc|clang)$ ]]; then
   138→        echo -e "Error! Pleas set the compiler to an appropriate one.\n" \
   139→                "The available compilers are:\n" \
   140→                "    riscv64-elf-gcc\n" \
   141→                "    riscv64-elf-clang"
   142→        touch "../${ERROR_LOG}"
   143→        exit 1
   144→    fi
   145→else
   146→    # here will not be reached
   147→    echo "Error! ${1} is not a supported CPU !"
   148→fi
   149→
   150→# use the customized linker script for moving the data into local memory
   151→if [[ ${CPU_NAME} =~ ^(n225|d23|n25|a25|d25|nx25|ax25|a27|n45|a45|d45|nx45|ax45|a46mp|ax46mp)$ ]]; then
   152→    CPU_FLAGS+=" -DENA_LM"
   153→fi
   154→
   155→# select the library to be linkd
   156→if [[ ${CPU_NAME} =~ ^(nx27v|ax45mpv|a46mpv|ax46mpv|ax66)$ ]]; then
   157→    CPU_FLAGS+=" -lnn_v"
   158→elif [[ ${CPU_NAME} =~ ^(nx27v|ax45mpv|ax46mpv|ax66)_elen64$ ]]; then
   159→    CPU_FLAGS+=" -lnn_v_elen64"
   160→elif [[ ${CPU_NAME} =~ ^(nx27v|ax45mpv|ax46mpv|ax66)_elen64_felen64$ ]]; then
   161→    CPU_FLAGS+=" -lnn_v_elen64_felen64"
   162→elif [[ ${CPU_NAME} =~ ^(ax45mpv|a46mpv|ax46mpv|ax66)_seg$ ]]; then
   163→    CPU_FLAGS+=" -lnn_v_seg"
   164→elif [[ ${CPU_NAME} =~ ^(ax45mpv|ax46mpv|ax66)_elen64_seg$ ]]; then
   165→    CPU_FLAGS+=" -lnn_v_elen64_seg"
   166→elif [[ ${CPU_NAME} =~ ^(ax45mpv|ax46mpv|ax66)_elen64_felen64_seg$ ]]; then
   167→    CPU_FLAGS+=" -lnn_v_elen64_felen64_seg"
   168→else
   169→    CPU_FLAGS+=" -lnn"
   170→fi
   171→
   172→# select the running on board wrapper
   173→if [[ ${CPU_NAME} =~ ^ax45mpv(_elen64(_felen64)?(_seg)?|_seg|_purec)?$ ]]; then
   174→   SIM_WRAPPER=$(readlink -f ${LIB_ROOT}/project/ax45mpv/riscv64-sim-wrapper-on-board)
   175→elif [[ ${CPU_NAME} =~ ^nx27v(_purec|_elen64|_elen64_felen64)?$ ]]; then
   176→   SIM_WRAPPER=$(readlink -f ${LIB_ROOT}/project/nx27v/riscv64-sim-wrapper-on-board)
   177→elif [[ ${CPU_NAME} =~ ^(n|a)x(25|45)$ ]] ||
   178→     [[ ${CPU_NAME} == ax46mp ]]; then
   179→   SIM_WRAPPER=$(readlink -f ${LIB_ROOT}/project/ax25/riscv64-sim-wrapper-on-board)
   180→elif [[ ${CPU_NAME} =~ ^a46mpv(_seg|_purec)?$  ]]; then
   181→   SIM_WRAPPER=$(readlink -f ${LIB_ROOT}/project/a46mpv/riscv32-sim-wrapper-on-board)
   182→elif [[ ${CPU_NAME} =~ ^ax46mpv(_elen64(_felen64)?(_seg)?|_seg|_purec)?$ ]]; then
   183→   SIM_WRAPPER=$(readlink -f ${LIB_ROOT}/project/ax46mpv/riscv64-sim-wrapper-on-board)
   184→elif [[ ${CPU_NAME} == ax65 ]]; then
   185→   SIM_WRAPPER=$(readlink -f ${LIB_ROOT}/project/ax65/riscv64-sim-wrapper-on-board)
   186→elif [[ ${CPU_NAME} =~ ^ax66(_elen64(_felen64)?(_seg)?|_seg|_purec)?$ ]]; then
   187→   SIM_WRAPPER=$(readlink -f ${LIB_ROOT}/project/ax66/riscv64-sim-wrapper-on-board)
   188→else
   189→   SIM_WRAPPER=$(readlink -f ${LIB_ROOT}/project/d25/riscv32-sim-wrapper-on-board)
   190→fi
   191→
   192→# -----
   193→# Automatically enable the zfh extension if the toolcahin supports it.
   194→# Note-1. We should check whether the zfh extension could be enabled with
   195→# all compilation flags. The zfh extension may be supported by the
   196→# toolchain; however, it's disabled by some compilation flags.
   197→# Note-2. Now the zfh extension is supported only on NX27V and 45-series
   198→# cores.
   199→# -----
   200→# check whether the zfh extension could be enabled
   201→${COMPILER_NAME} -mzfh -E -dM ${CPU_FLAGS} - < /dev/null &> /dev/null
   202→if [ $? -eq 0 ]; then
   203→    # check whether the toolchain is [single|double]-abi
   204→    # (for the spcial case of v5 toolchain + clang)
   205→    ${COMPILER_NAME} -mzfh -E -dM ${CPU_FLAGS} - < /dev/null | grep '__riscv_float_abi_single\|__riscv_float_abi_double' &> /dev/null
   206→    if [ $? -eq 0 ]; then
   207→        # check whether the core is NX27V/45-series/46-series/60-series
   208→        if [[ ${CPU_NAME} =~ (nx27v|45|46|65|66) ]]; then
   209→            CPU_FLAGS="${CPU_FLAGS} -mzfh"
   210→        fi
   211→    fi
   212→fi
   213→
   214→##### main ######
   215→rm -f ${TEST_LOG}
   216→log "***************************************************************************************"
   217→log ${LIB_NAME} performance testing begin ...
   218→log "Input 1(CPU): ${CPU_NAME}"
   219→log "Input 2(COMPILER): ${COMPILER_NAME}"
   220→log "Input 3(TEST_TARGET): ${TARGET}"
   221→log "SIM_WRAPPER: ${SIM_WRAPPER}"
   222→log "EXTRA_CFLAGS: ${CPU_FLAGS}"
   223→log "***************************************************************************************"
   224→
   225→EXTRA_CFLAGS="${CPU_FLAGS}"
   226→${LIB_ROOT}/test.sh "" "${COMPILER_NAME}" "${SIM_WRAPPER}" "${TARGET}" "BS3" "${EXTRA_CFLAGS} -DENA_TEST_PERF -DENA_DUMP_PARAMETERS"
   227→
   228→log "***************************************************************************************"
   229→log ${LIB_NAME} performance testing end.
   230→log "***************************************************************************************"
   231→
   232→# move the log int test_sh_<TIMESTAMP> folder
   233→out_folder_list=($(find . -name "test_sh*" -type d | sort))
   234→mv "test_perf.log" -t "${out_folder_list[${#arr[@]}-1]}"
   235→

1→#!/usr/bin/env bash
     2→################################################################################
     3→# Input
     4→# $1: toolcahin prefix
     5→# $2: the wrapper to execute the application
     6→# $3: extra flags
     7→################################################################################
     8→
     9→#######################################################
    10→# run the unit test and check the accuracy results
    11→#   $1: test function name
    12→#   $2: test function group name (for grouping excel results)
    13→#######################################################
    14→function run_and_check {
    15→    # skip the fp16 functions when the zfh extension is not enabled
    16→    if [[ ${ENA_FP16} -eq 0 ]] && [[ "${1}" == *"f16"* ]]; then
    17→        unsupport_num=$((unsupport_num + 1))
    18→
    19→        if [ ${ENA_GREP_PF_TO_CSV} == 1 ]; then
    20→            echo "$2,$1,x,x," >> ${CSV_OUT}
    21→        fi
    22→        return 0
    23→    fi
    24→
    25→    # skip the integer 4-bit functions when the v-ext enabled but the VL4 not enabled
    26→    if [[ ${CFLAGS} =~ -mext-vector ]] && [[ ${ENA_VL4} -eq 0 ]] && [[ "${1}" == *"s4"* ]]; then
    27→        unsupport_num=$((unsupport_num + 1))
    28→
    29→        if [ ${ENA_GREP_PF_TO_CSV} == 1 ]; then
    30→            echo "$2,$1,x,x," >> ${CSV_OUT}
    31→        fi
    32→        return 0
    33→    fi
    34→
    35→    echo "$1"
    36→    log="${LOG_DIR}/${1}.log"
    37→    build_cmd="${CC} ${CFLAGS} ${MACRO} ${INC_DIR} -o ${ADX_DIR}/${TEST_SOURCE_PREFIX}${1}.adx ${TEST_SOURCE_DIR}/${TEST_SOURCE_PREFIX}${1}.c ${LINKED_LIB} -lm"
    38→    set -x
    39→        ${build_cmd}
    40→        local ret=${?}  #check the compiling successful or not
    41→        if [ ${ret} == 0 ]; then
    42→            # dump the disassembly
    43→            ${OBJDUMP} "${ADX_DIR}/${TEST_SOURCE_PREFIX}${1}.adx" -S > "${OBJDUMP_DIR}/${TEST_SOURCE_PREFIX}${1}.objdump"
    44→
    45→            echo "Start at $(date)"
    46→            ${SIM_WRAPPER} ${ADX_DIR}/${TEST_SOURCE_PREFIX}${1}.adx 2>&1 | tee ${log}
    47→            ret=${PIPESTATUS[0]}
    48→            echo "End at $(date)"
    49→        fi
    50→    set +x
    51→
    52→    if [ ${ret} == 0 ]; then
    53→        # check whehter the program is executed (For the case of AndeSim
    54→        # doesn't launch successfully. BTW, AndeSim doesn't return error
    55→        # code even if it enounters some issues.)
    56→        # Here we use the ALWAYS present string as the criteria whether
    57→        # the test program is executed.
    58→        grep -w 'pass\|fail' ${log} &> /dev/null
    59→        if [ $? -ne 0 ]; then
    60→            fail_list+=("${1}")
    61→            echo "Warning! There is no 'pass' or 'fail' string is parsed in this log file; therefore, the program may not executed." >> ${log}
    62→        else
    63→            pass_num=$((pass_num + 1))
    64→        fi
    65→
    66→        if [ ${ENA_GREP_PF_TO_CSV} == 1 ]; then
    67→            #dump inst/cycle into csv
    68→            echo -e "$2,$1,\c" >> ${CSV_OUT}
    69→            grep -w "inst" ${log} | awk '{printf "%ld,", $5}' >> ${CSV_OUT}
    70→            grep -w "cycle count" ${log} | awk '{printf "%ld,", $5}' >> ${CSV_OUT}
    71→            #grep -w "miss stall" ${log} | awk '{printf "%ld,", $7}' >> ${CSV_OUT}
    72→            echo >> ${CSV_OUT}  #new line
    73→        fi
    74→    else
    75→        fail_list+=("${1}")
    76→    fi
    77→}
    78→
    79→################################################################################
    80→#                                   main                                       #
    81→################################################################################
    82→ENA_TEST_ALL=1
    83→ENA_ACTIVATION_TEST=0
    84→ENA_BASIC_TEST=0
    85→ENA_CONCATE_TEST=0
    86→ENA_CONV_TEST=0
    87→ENA_FC_TEST=0
    88→ENA_POOLING_TEST=0
    89→ENA_SOFTMAX_TEST=0
    90→ENA_UTIL_TEST=0
    91→
    92→ENA_DUMP_GOLDEN=0           # only for the test programs with DYNAMIC allocation buffers
    93→ENA_MEASURE_PERF=0          # only run large test case to measure the performance (
    94→                            # run the program with DYNAMIC allocation buffers)
    95→ENA_GREP_PF_TO_CSV=0        # grep instruction and cycle counts into csv
    96→ENA_MEASURE_PERF_STATIC=0   # run the test prgram with STATIC allocation buffers
    97→ENA_RANDOM_INPUT=0          # The testers can turn on this switch as running the
    98→                            # test programs in the environment not supporting
    99→                            # FILE I/O, e.g. m4 or m7. The inputs will be randomly
   100→                            # generated and the results will not be verified.

1→#!/usr/bin/env bash
     2→################################################################################
     3→# Input:
     4→#  $1: extra compilation flags or macro for both libray and application
     5→################################################################################
     6→CWD=$(pwd)
     7→
     8→#----- set up the tool name -----
     9→source ../NLIB_CROSS_V5_RV64
    10→
    11→#----- build and run switches -----
    12→ENA_BUILD_LIBNN=1
    13→ENA_RUN_CIFAR10=0
    14→ENA_RUN_GRU=0
    15→ENA_RUN_PNET=0
    16→ENA_RUN_MTCNN=0
    17→ENA_RUN_MTCNN_DW=0
    18→ENA_RUN_DW_TEST=0
    19→ENA_RUN_DW_NONSQ_TEST=0
    20→ENA_RUN_UNIT_FUNC=1
    21→RUN_CONFIG=2        #0: run app on board, 1: run app on SID, 2: run app on QEMU
    22→ENA_CLANG=0         #0: use gcc as compiler, 1: use clang as compiler
    23→ENA_RUN_TWICE=0     #0: run function once, 1: run function twice
    24→ENA_VEC_ISA=1       #0: disable v-ext (for measuring plain C algo performance), 1: enable v-ext
    25→ENA_VDOT=1          #0: use the default v-ext algorithm, 1: use vdot v-ext algorithm
    26→ENA_VEC_INTRIN=0    #0: using the inline assembly version v-ext algorithm, 1: use the intrinsic version v-ext algorithm
    27→ENA_HVM=0           #0: place the program's data on cache, 1: place the program's data on HVM
    28→ENA_HPM=0           #0: disable the performance monitor, 1: enable the performance monitor
    29→ENA_ACE_RVV=0       #0: disabe the ACE-RVV, 1: enable the ACE-RVV
    30→ENA_ZVLSSEG=0       #0: disable zvlsseg extension, 1: enable zvlsseg extension
    31→ENA_VL4=1           #0: disable VL4 extension, 1: enable VL4 extension
    32→VEC_CONFIG=4        #0: zve32x, 1: zve32f, 2: zve64x, 3: zve64f, 4: zv64d
    33→
    34→#----- flags of library and app -----
    35→CPU_NAME="ax45mpv"
    36→CPU_FLAG="-mtune=andes-45-series"
    37→APP_INCLUDE_PATH="-I./../../Examples/unit_func/include -I./../../Include -I./../../internal"
    38→LIB_INCLUDE_PATH="-I./Include -I./internal"
    39→if [[ ${ENA_VEC_ISA} -eq 1 ]]; then
    40→    if [[ ${VEC_CONFIG} -eq 0 ]]; then
    41→        VEC_CFG="zve32x"
    42→    elif [[ ${VEC_CONFIG} -eq 1 ]]; then
    43→        VEC_CFG="zve32f"
    44→    elif [[ ${VEC_CONFIG} -eq 2 ]]; then
    45→        VEC_CFG="zve64x"
    46→    elif [[ ${VEC_CONFIG} -eq 3 ]]; then
    47→        VEC_CFG="zve64f"
    48→    else
    49→        VEC_CFG="zve64d"
    50→    fi
    51→
    52→    CPU_FLAG+=" -mext-vector=${VEC_CFG} -DNDS_VEC_RVV_VERSION=1000"
    53→    # export the VEC_CFG environment variable for selecting the proper QEMU conf
    54→    export VEC_CFG="${VEC_CFG}"
    55→fi
    56→if [[ ${ENA_VEC_ISA} -eq 1 ]] && [[ ${ENA_VDOT} -eq 0 ]]; then
    57→    CPU_FLAG+=" -UENA_NDS_V5_VEC_DOT_PROD"
    58→fi
    59→if [[ ${ENA_VEC_ISA} -eq 1 ]] && [[ ${ENA_ZVLSSEG} -eq 1 ]]; then
    60→    CPU_FLAG+=" -DENA_VEC_ISA_ZVLSSEG"
    61→fi
    62→if [[ ${ENA_VEC_ISA} -eq 1 ]] && [[ ${ENA_CLANG} -eq 1 ]] && [[ ${ENA_VEC_INTRIN} -eq 1 ]]; then
    63→    # please note that now only clang supports RVV intrinsic
    64→    CPU_FLAG+=" -DENA_VEC_INTRINSIC -fno-unroll-loops"
    65→fi
    66→COMMON_CFLAG="${CPU_FLAG} -O3 -Wall -Werror -ffunction-sections -fdata-sections -DPF_COUNTER ${1}"    #shared flags for library ad app
    67→if [[ ${ENA_ACE_RVV} -eq 1 ]]; then

#######################################################
# collect the code size of each section
#   $1: test function name
#######################################################
calculate_code_size () {
    local log_file build_cmd tmp_size_log

    echo -n "${1}," >> ${SIZE_CSV}
    log_file="${LOG_DIR}/${1}_empty.log"
    build_cmd="${CC} ${DEFAULT_APP_CFLAGS} -O3 ${EXTRA_FLAG} -DENA_DUMP_GOLDEN=${ENA_DUMP_GOLDEN} -DENA_MEASURE_PERF=${ENA_MEASURE_PERF} -DGETENV_TESTDIR=${UNIT_TEST_PATH} ${INC_DIR} -o ${ADX_DIR}/t_$1_empty.adx ${UNIT_TEST_PATH}/t_$1.c ${EMPTY_LIB} -lm"
    #use the linker specified by the user
    if [[ -n ${LD} ]]; then
        build_cmd="${build_cmd} -fuse-ld=${LD}"
    fi

    # build the executable
    ${build_cmd} 2>>${log_file} | tee -a ${log_file}

    # get the size info
    tmp_size_log="${LOG_DIR}/size.log"
    ${SIZE} ${ADX_DIR}/t_${1}.adx       | tail -1 > ${tmp_size_log}
    ${SIZE} ${ADX_DIR}/t_${1}_empty.adx | tail -1 >> ${tmp_size_log}

    # calculate the size of each section
    awk '
        NR==1 {split($0,non_empty)}
        NR==2 {split($0,empty)}
        END {for(i=1;i<=5;i++) printf "%u,", non_empty[i] - empty[i]}
    ' ${tmp_size_log} >> ${SIZE_CSV}
    echo >> ${SIZE_CSV}  #new line
}

#######################################################
# run the unit test and check the accuracy results
#   $1: test function name
#   $2: test function group name (for grouping excel results)
#######################################################
function run_and_check {
    # skip the fp16 functions when the zfh extension is not enabled
    if [ ${ENA_FP16} -eq 0 ] && [[ "${1}" == *"f16"* ]]; then
        accuracy_unsupport_num=$((accuracy_unsupport_num + 1))
        echo "$2,$1,x,x," >> ${PERF_CSV}
        return
    fi

    # skip the integer 4-bit functions when the v-ext enabled but the VLN8 not enabled
    if [[ ${EXTRA_FLAG} =~ -mext-vector ]] && [[ ${ENA_VL4} -eq 0 ]] && [[ ${1} =~ s4 ]]; then
        accuracy_unsupport_num=$((accuracy_unsupport_num + 1))
        echo "$2,$1,x,x," >> ${PERF_CSV}
        return
    fi

    log "--- $1 ---"
    local log_file="${LOG_DIR}/${1}.log"
    build_cmd="${CC} ${DEFAULT_APP_CFLAGS} -O3 -DENA_DUMP_GOLDEN=${ENA_DUMP_GOLDEN} -DENA_MEASURE_PERF=${ENA_MEASURE_PERF} -DGETENV_TESTDIR=${UNIT_TEST_PATH} ${INC_DIR} -o ${ADX_DIR}/t_$1.adx ${UNIT_TEST_PATH}/t_$1.c ${LINKED_LIB} ${EXTRA_FLAG} -lm"
    #use the linker specified by the user
    if [[ -n ${LD} ]]; then
        build_cmd="${build_cmd} -fuse-ld=${LD}"
    fi

    echo ${build_cmd} > ${log_file}
    ${build_cmd} 2>>${log_file} | tee -a ${log_file}
    if [ ${PIPESTATUS[0]} == 0 ]; then  #check the compiling successful or not
        # dump the disassembly
        ${OBJDUMP} "${ADX_DIR}/t_${1}.adx" -S > "${OBJDUMP_DIR}/t_${1}.objdump"

        log "Start at $(date)"
        ${SIM_WRAPPER} ${ADX_DIR}/t_$1.adx >> ${log_file} 2>&1
        ret=$?
        log "End at $(date)"
