content.content:
#note. the code coverage testing is not supported on board
if [ "${TEST_TYPE}" == "DAILY" ] && [[ ${TEST_TARGET} =~ ^(SID|QEMU)$ ]] && [[ "${CC}" != *"clang" ]] && [[ ${ENA_MEASURE_CODE_SIZE} -eq 0 ]]; then
    DEFAULT_LIB_CFLAGS="${DEFAULT_LIB_CFLAGS} --coverage -DGCOV"
    CODE_COVERAGE="1"
else
    CODE_COVERAGE="0"
fi

DEFAULT_APP_CFLAGS="${DEFAULT_LIB_CFLAGS} -Wl,-gc-sections"
if [[ ${CC} =~ "elf" ]]; then
    # only enable the performance counter for elf toolcahins
    DEFAULT_APP_CFLAGS+=" -DPF_COUNTER"

    # enable virtual hosting / semihosting
    if [[ ${TEST_TARGET} =~ ^(BOARD|QEMU|ALIOTH)$ ]]; then
        DEFAULT_APP_CFLAGS="${DEFAULT_APP_CFLAGS} -mvh"
    fi
fi

if [[ ${TEST_TARGET} == "BOARD" ]] && [[ ${CC} =~ "elf" ]]; then
    if [[ ${EXTRA_FLAG} = *"-DENA_QILAI_LD"* ]]; then
        # applying the customized linker script for Qilai platform
        DEFAULT_APP_CFLAGS="${DEFAULT_APP_CFLAGS} -Wl,-T ${LIB_ROOT_PATH}/project/nx27v_qilai/qilai.ld"
    elif [[ "${CC}" == "nds32"* ]]; then
        # move rodata to LM with the customized linker script (for V3)
        DEFAULT_APP_CFLAGS="${DEFAULT_APP_CFLAGS} -Wl,-T ${LIB_ROOT_PATH}/project/d10/nds32.ld"
    elif [[ "${CC}" == "riscv"* ]] && [[ ${EXTRA_FLAG} != *"-mext-vector"* ]] && [[ ${EXTRA_FLAG} =~ "ENA_LM" ]] ; then
        # move rodata to LM with the customized linker script (for V5)
        DEFAULT_APP_CFLAGS="${DEFAULT_APP_CFLAGS} -Wl,-T ${LIB_ROOT_PATH}/project/d25/ae350-xip.ld"
    fi
fi

# app and lib cflags for v-ext
if [[ ${EXTRA_FLAG} = *"-mext-vector"* ]]; then
    DEFAULT_LIB_CFLAGS="${DEFAULT_LIB_CFLAGS} -DENA_VEC_ISA -DMAX_VLEN=1024 -fno-tree-slp-vectorize -fno-tree-vectorize"
    if [[ ${CC} =~ "elf" ]]; then
        DEFAULT_APP_CFLAGS="${DEFAULT_APP_CFLAGS} -DENA_FS_VS"
    fi

    # As using v-ext, now the fast algorithm will be applied by default
    DEFAULT_APP_CFLAGS="${DEFAULT_APP_CFLAGS} -DENA_FAST_ALGO"

    if [[ ${CC} =~ "elf" ]]; then
        # As measuring v-ext algorithm on cacheable environment, we can enable
        # ENA_CACHEABLE_ENV_SETUP switch to get better performance.
        DEFAULT_APP_CFLAGS="${DEFAULT_APP_CFLAGS} -DENA_CACHEABLE_ENV_SETUP"
    fi
fi

# extra cflags for the environment with HVM
if [[ ${EXTRA_FLAG} = *"-DENA_HVM"* ]]; then
    DEFAULT_LIB_CFLAGS="${DEFAULT_LIB_CFLAGS} -mcmodel=large"
    DEFAULT_APP_CFLAGS="${DEFAULT_APP_CFLAGS} -mcmodel=large"
fi

# setup the extra flags for the glibc toolchain
if [[ ${CC} =~ "linux" ]] && [[ ! ${EXTRA_FLAG} =~ "-static" ]]; then
    DEFAULT_LIB_CFLAGS+=" -fpic"
fi

# customized crt0 for v-ext 0.8
if [[ ${EXTRA_FLAG} = *"-mext-vector"* ]] && [[ ${EXTRA_FLAG} = *"NDS_VEC_RVV_VERSION=80"* ]]; then
    DEFAULT_APP_CFLAGS="${DEFAULT_APP_CFLAGS} -nostartfiles ${LIB_ROOT_PATH}/project/nx25v/crt0.s"
fi

#header files and their search path
if [[ ${TEST_TYPE} == BS3 ]]; then
    # As verifying the unit tests with BS3, we should use the header files is
    # the used toolchain instead of those in libnn repo.
    TOOLCHAIN_PATH=$(which ${CC})
    if [[ ${CC} == *elf* ]]; then
        # for elf toolchain
        PREFIX=${CC%-*}
        INC_PATH_IN_TOOLCHAIN=$(readlink -f "${TOOLCHAIN_PATH%/*}/../${PREFIX}/include")
    else
        # for linux toolchain
        INC_PATH_IN_TOOLCHAIN=$(readlink -f "${TOOLCHAIN_PATH%/*}/../sysroot/usr/include")
    fi
    INC_DIR="-I${INC_PATH_IN_TOOLCHAIN} -I${UNIT_TEST_PATH}/include"
else
    # As verifying the unit tests during developing, we should use the header
    # files in libnn repo; otherwise, the prototypes of new functions can't
    # be found by the compiler.
    INC_DIR="-I${LIB_ROOT_PATH}/Include -I${UNIT_TEST_PATH}/include"
fi

#Some identification flags
if [[ ${CC} == "nds32be"* ]]; then
    ENDIAN="be"
else
    ENDIAN="le"
fi

if [[ ${CC} == *"linux"* ]]; then
    TOOLCHAIN_IS_GLIBC="1"
else
    TOOLCHAIN_IS_GLIBC="0"
fi

#select the library to be linked (from built-in or manual building library)
if [ "${TEST_TYPE}" == "BS3" ];then

##################################################
# Build the non-empty library.
##################################################
if [[ ${TEST_TYPE} == DAILY ]]; then
    build_lib "${DEFAULT_LIB_CFLAGS} ${EXTRA_FLAG}"
fi

##################################################
# start to execute the unit tests
##################################################
if [ ${build_lib_result} == 0 ]; then
    log
    log "****************************************************"
    log "Begin the accuracy and performance testing ..."
    log

    #---- enable the configuration diagnosis program for ELF toolcahins -----
    if [ ${ENA_TEST_ALL} == 1 ] && [[ ! ${CC} == *linux* ]]; then
        target_cfg_diagnosis
    fi

    #TODO: the inst/cycle field needs to be mofiled (they may not show just one time)
    if [ ${ENA_GREP_PF_TO_CSV} == 1 ]; then
        #init csv title
        if [ ${ENA_MEASURE_PERF} == 1 ]; then
            echo "group, function, inst, cycle" > ${PERF_CSV}
        fi
    fi

    if [ ${ENA_GREP_PARAM_TO_CSV} == 1 ]; then
        echo "group, function, parameters" > ${PARAM_CSV}
    fi

    if [[ ${ENA_MEASURE_CODE_SIZE} -eq 1 ]]; then
        # init the csv title
        echo "fun_name,text,code,rodata,data,bss" > ${SIZE_CSV}
    fi

    #----- enable the LM switch -----
    if [[ ${EXTRA_FLAG} =~ ENA_LM ]]; then
        sed -i 's/CACHE="on"/CACHE="lm"/' ${SIM_WRAPPER}
    fi

    for item in "${test_list[@]}"; do
        fun_name=$(echo ${item} | cut -d ':' -f1)
        fun_group=$(echo ${item} | cut -d ':' -f2)

        if [[ ${CC} == *linux* ]] && [[ ${TEST_TARGET} == BOARD ]]; then
            # As using the linux toolchain and the test target is board, this
            # script is only used to generate the executables.
            build_executable "${fun_name}"

            # copy the required binary files for the unit tests
            cp -r ${UNIT_TEST_PATH}/bin ${TEST_LOG_DIR}

            # copy the running executables for glibc on board script
            cp ${LIB_ROOT_PATH}/run_executable_for_glibc.sh ${TEST_LOG_DIR}

            # enable the write permission for others
            chmod o+w ${TEST_LOG_DIR}
        else

content.content:
include ../NLIB_CROSS_V5_RV64

APP_SOURCE_DIR = ../../Examples/ARM/arm_nn_examples
APP_INCLUDE_DIR = -I../../Examples/ARM/arm_nn_examples
PNET_SOURCE_DIR = ../../Examples/ML-PNET
PNET_INCLUDE_DIR = -I../../Examples/ML-PNET
MTCNN_SOURCE_DIR = ../../Examples/ML-MTCNN
MTCNN_INCLUDE_DIR = -I../../Examples/ML-MTCNN
DW_TEST_SOURCE_DIR = ../../Examples/dw_test
DW_NONSQ_TEST_SOURCE_DIR = ../../Examples/dw_nonsq_test
SHARED_INCLUDE_DIR = -I./../Include -I./../../Include -I./../../internal
NN_LIB = ../../libnn.a
DSP_LIB= -ldsp

PNET_APP_NAME = pnet
CIFAR10_APP_NAME = cifar10
GRU_APP_NAME = gru
MTCNN_APP_NAME = mtcnn
DW_TEST_APP_NAME = dw_test
DW_NONSQ_TEST_APP_NAME = dw_nonsq_test

ifneq (${CFLAGS},)
    CF = ${CFLAGS}
else
    #CF = -mcpu=nx25 -O3 -ffunction-sections -fdata-sections -DPF_COUNTER -fno-strict-aliasing ${DSP_LIB} $(CFLAGS) -mvh -T./ae350-xip.ld
    CF = -mcpu=nx25 -O3 -ffunction-sections -fdata-sections -DPF_COUNTER -fno-strict-aliasing ${DSP_LIB} $(CFLAGS) -DFAST_VERSION -DFUNCTION_PF_ANALYZE
endif
LF = -Wl,--gc-sections -lm ${NN_LIB} ${DSP_LIB} ${SHARED_INCLUDE_DIR}

pnet:
	@echo "Building PNET..."
	$(CC) ${PNET_INCLUDE_DIR} -o $(PNET_APP_NAME).adx ${PNET_SOURCE_DIR}/PNetmain.c \
	-Wl,-Map=./$(PNET_APP_NAME).map,--cref $(CF) $(LF) \
	-std=c99 1>$(PNET_APP_NAME)_make.log 2>$(PNET_APP_NAME)_make_warning.log
	@rm -f $(PNET_APP_NAME).size
	@${SZ} $(PNET_APP_NAME).adx | tee $(PNET_APP_NAME).size
	@echo "Generating objdump files..."
	${OBJDUMP} -S $(PNET_APP_NAME).adx > $(PNET_APP_NAME).objdump
	@echo

cifar10:
	@echo "Building CIFAR10..."
	$(CC) $(APP_INCLUDE_DIR)/cifar10/ -o $(CIFAR10_APP_NAME).adx \
	${APP_SOURCE_DIR}/cifar10/nds_nn_examples_cifar10.c -Wl,-Map=./$(CIFAR10_APP_NAME).map,--cref \
	$(CF) $(LF) 1>$(CIFAR10_APP_NAME)_make.log 2>$(CIFAR10_APP_NAME)_make_warning.log
	@rm -f $(CIFAR10_APP_NAME).size
	@${SZ} $(CIFAR10_APP_NAME).adx | tee $(CIFAR10_APP_NAME).size
	@echo "Generating objdump files..."
	${OBJDUMP} -S $(CIFAR10_APP_NAME).adx > $(CIFAR10_APP_NAME).objdump
	@echo

gru:
	@echo "Building GRU..."
	$(CC) $(APP_INCLUDE_DIR)/gru/ -o $(GRU_APP_NAME).adx ${APP_SOURCE_DIR}/gru/nds_nn_examples_gru.c \
	-Wl,-Map=./$(GRU_APP_NAME).map,--cref $(CF) $(LF) \
	1>$(GRU_APP_NAME)_make.log 2>$(GRU_APP_NAME)_make_warning.log
	@rm -f $(GRU_APP_NAME).size
	@${SZ} $(GRU_APP_NAME).adx | tee $(GRU_APP_NAME).size
	@echo "Generating objdump files..."
	${OBJDUMP} -S $(GRU_APP_NAME).adx > $(GRU_APP_NAME).objdump
	@echo

mtcnn:
	@echo "Building MTCNN ..."
	$(CC) ${MTCNN_INCLUDE_DIR} -o $(MTCNN_APP_NAME).adx ${MTCNN_SOURCE_DIR}/PNetmain.c \
	${MTCNN_SOURCE_DIR}/RNetmain.c ${MTCNN_SOURCE_DIR}/ONetmain.c ${MTCNN_SOURCE_DIR}/MTCNN_function.c \
	-Wl,-Map=./$(MTCNN_APP_NAME).map,--cref $(CF) $(LF) -std=c99 -mcmodel=large \
	1>$(MTCNN_APP_NAME)_make.log 2>$(MTCNN_APP_NAME)_make_warning.log
	@rm -f $(MTCNN_APP_NAME).size
	@${SZ} $(MTCNN_APP_NAME).adx | tee $(MTCNN_APP_NAME).size
	@echo "Generating objdump files..."
	${OBJDUMP} -S $(MTCNN_APP_NAME).adx > $(MTCNN_APP_NAME).objdump
	@echo

dw_test:
	@echo "Building DW_TEST ..."
	$(CC) -o $(DW_TEST_APP_NAME).adx ${DW_TEST_SOURCE_DIR}/RNetmain.cpp \
	-Wl,-Map=./$(DW_TEST_APP_NAME).map,--cref $(CF) $(LF) \
	1>$(DW_TEST_APP_NAME)_make.log 2>$(DW_TEST_APP_NAME)_make_warning.log
	@rm -f $(DW_TEST_APP_NAME).size
	@${SZ} $(DW_TEST_APP_NAME).adx | tee $(DW_TEST_APP_NAME).size
	@echo "Generating objdump files..."
	${OBJDUMP} -S $(DW_TEST_APP_NAME).adx > $(DW_TEST_APP_NAME).objdump
	@echo

dw_nonsq_test:
	@echo "Building DW_NONSQ_TEST ..."
	$(CC) -o $(DW_NONSQ_TEST_APP_NAME).adx ${DW_NONSQ_TEST_SOURCE_DIR}/PNetmain.cpp \
	-Wl,-Map=./$(DW_TEST_APP_NAME).map,--cref $(CF) $(LF) \
	1>$(DW_NONSQ_TEST_APP_NAME)_make.log 2>$(DW_NONSQ_TEST_APP_NAME)_make_warning.log
	@rm -f $(DW_NONSQ_TEST_APP_NAME).size
	@${SZ} $(DW_NONSQ_TEST_APP_NAME).adx | tee $(DW_NONSQ_TEST_APP_NAME).size
	@echo "Generating objdump files..."
	${OBJDUMP} -S $(DW_NONSQ_TEST_APP_NAME).adx > $(DW_NONSQ_TEST_APP_NAME).objdump
	@echo

all: pnet cifar10 gru mtcnn dw_test dw_nonsq_test

clean:
	rm -f *.objdump *.adx *.size *.log *.map

#----- EDITABLE switches end -----

#----- NON-EDITABLE global variables begin -----
# lib name
LIB_NAME="libnn"

#Path info
CURRENT_PATH=$(pwd)                                 #position of user invokes this script
LIB_ROOT_PATH=$(readlink -f `dirname ${0}`)         #position of libnn repository root folder
WRAPPTER_PATH=$(readlink -f ${CURRENT_PATH}/..)     #the wrapper position of BS3
UNIT_TEST_PATH="${LIB_ROOT_PATH}/Examples/unit_func"
#export each cpu project here to make sure the sim wrapper could be found in daily test ???
# export PATH=$( find ${LIB_ROOT_PATH}/project/ -type d -printf ":%p" ):$PATH

#Output files/folders
TIME_STR=$(date +%Y%m%d%H%M%S)
TEST_LOG="test_sh_${TIME_STR}.log"      #save the ouput message of this script.
TEST_LOG_DIR="test_sh_${TIME_STR}"
PERF_CSV="${CURRENT_PATH}/${TEST_LOG_DIR}/performance.csv"
PARAM_CSV="${CURRENT_PATH}/${TEST_LOG_DIR}/parameter.csv"
LOG_DIR="${CURRENT_PATH}/${TEST_LOG_DIR}/log"
ADX_DIR="${CURRENT_PATH}/${TEST_LOG_DIR}/adx"           # collect the executables into TEST_LOG_DIR
OBJDUMP_DIR="${CURRENT_PATH}/${TEST_LOG_DIR}/objdump"   # collect the disassembly into TEST_LOG_DIR
GCOV_DIR="${CURRENT_PATH}/${TEST_LOG_DIR}/gcov"         # temporary folder to keep gcov outputs
FAILED_GCOV_DIR="${CURRENT_PATH}/${TEST_LOG_DIR}/faild_gcov"
ERROR_LOG="diff_log"
ACCURACY_FAIL_LIST="${CURRENT_PATH}/${TEST_LOG_DIR}/accuracy_fail_list"

ENA_RUN_CIFAR10=0
ENA_RUN_GRU=0
ENA_RUN_PNET=0
ENA_RUN_MTCNN=0
ENA_RUN_MTCNN_DW=0
ENA_RUN_DW_TEST=0
ENA_RUN_DW_NONSQ_TEST=0
if [ ${ENA_RUN_CIFAR10} -eq 1 ];then
if [ ${ENA_RUN_GRU} -eq 1 ];then
if [ ${ENA_RUN_PNET} -eq 1 ];then
if [ ${ENA_RUN_MTCNN} -eq 1 ];then
if [ ${ENA_RUN_MTCNN_DW} -eq 1 ];then
if [ ${ENA_RUN_DW_TEST} -eq 1 ];then
if [ ${ENA_RUN_DW_NONSQ_TEST} -eq 1 ];then

#   $1: test function name
#   $2: test function group name (for grouping excel results)
#######################################################
function run_and_check {
    # skip the fp16 functions when the zfh extension is not enabled
    if [[ ${ENA_FP16} -eq 0 ]] && [[ "${1}" == *"f16"* ]]; then
        unsupport_num=$((unsupport_num + 1))

        if [ ${ENA_GREP_PF_TO_CSV} == 1 ]; then
            echo "$2,$1,x,x," >> ${CSV_OUT}
        fi
        return 0
    fi

    # skip the integer 4-bit functions when the v-ext enabled but the VL4 not enabled
    if [[ ${CFLAGS} =~ -mext-vector ]] && [[ ${ENA_VL4} -eq 0 ]] && [[ "${1}" == *"s4"* ]]; then
        unsupport_num=$((unsupport_num + 1))

        if [ ${ENA_GREP_PF_TO_CSV} == 1 ]; then
            echo "$2,$1,x,x," >> ${CSV_OUT}
        fi
        return 0
    fi

    echo "$1"
    log="${LOG_DIR}/${1}.log"
    build_cmd="${CC} ${CFLAGS} ${MACRO} ${INC_DIR} -o ${ADX_DIR}/${TEST_SOURCE_PREFIX}${1}.adx ${TEST_SOURCE_DIR}/${TEST_SOURCE_PREFIX}${1}.c ${LINKED_LIB} -lm"
    set -x
        ${build_cmd}
        local ret=${?}  #check the compiling successful or not
        if [ ${ret} == 0 ]; then
            # dump the disassembly
            ${OBJDUMP} "${ADX_DIR}/${TEST_SOURCE_PREFIX}${1}.adx" -S > "${OBJDUMP_DIR}/${TEST_SOURCE_PREFIX}${1}.objdump"

            echo "Start at $(date)"
            ${SIM_WRAPPER} ${ADX_DIR}/${TEST_SOURCE_PREFIX}${1}.adx 2>&1 | tee ${log}
            ret=${PIPESTATUS[0]}
            echo "End at $(date)"
        fi
    set +x

content.text:
Perfect! Now I have a comprehensive understanding. Let me create a detailed summary report:

## Test Infrastructure Summary for libnn

Based on my thorough exploration of the libnn project test infrastructure, here are all the distinct types and categories of testing available:

### 1. PRIMARY TEST MODES (test.sh)

There are **two main test execution modes** controlled by the 5th parameter to `test.sh`:

#### **BS3 Mode** (Built-in multi-lib)
- Uses pre-built multi-library configurations instead of custom compilation
- Verifies accuracy only
- Does NOT support code coverage or SAT extension testing
- Faster execution (uses compiled libraries)
- Used for quick validation

#### **DAILY Mode** (Development/Full testing)
- User specifies compiler and compilation options
- Builds library from source with custom flags
- Tests both accuracy AND code coverage (when running on SID/QEMU)
- Enables comprehensive validation
- Supports additional metrics collection
- Cannot measure code coverage on BOARD targets

### 2. UNIT FUNCTION TESTS (~610+ test functions)

Located in: `/local/nick/libnn/Examples/unit_func/`

**Test Organization:**
- 302 individual unit test files (`t_*.c`) covering all library functions
- Tests organized by 9 functional categories:
  1. **ACTIVATION** - ReLU, sigmoid, tanh, GELU, LeakyReLU, etc. (35+ functions)
  2. **BASIC** - Element-wise operations (add, mul, sub, broadcast) (21+ functions)
  3. **CONCATE** - Concatenation, slicing, padding, splitting (25+ functions)
  4. **CONV** - Convolution operations (1x1, depthwise, dilated) (70+ functions)
  5. **FC** - Fully connected layers (30+ functions)
  6. **POOLING** - Average/max pooling (20+ functions)
  7. **SOFTMAX** - Softmax variants (10+ functions)
  8. **UTIL** - Utilities (LSTM, quantize, transpose, reshape) (50+ functions)
  9. **SUPPORT** - Other support functions (20+ functions)

**Test Variants Per Function:**
Each function is tested with different precision types:
- `_s8` (8-bit quantized)
- `_s16` (16-bit quantized)
- `_s4` (4-bit quantized, when VL4 enabled)
- `_f16` (16-bit floating-point, requires zfh extension)
- `_f32` (32-bit floating-point)
- `_2buf` (2-buffer variants for in-place operations)
- `_asym` (asymmetric quantization)
- `_any` (any stride/padding variants)

### 3. UNIT TEST EXECUTION FLAGS

Within `Examples/unit_func/run.sh`, several independent test modes control what gets measured:

#### **Accuracy Testing** (Default)
- Verifies correct computation against golden reference data
- Compiles, runs, and checks output against expected results
- Supports dynamic and static buffer allocation tests

#### **Performance Measurement** (ENA_MEASURE_PERF=1)
- Measures instruction count and cycle count
- Uses large test cases for realistic performance profiling
- Outputs to CSV format: `group, function, inst, cycle`
- Used by `test_perf.sh`

#### **Performance Measurement (Static)** (ENA_MEASURE_PERF_STATIC=1)
- Runs performance tests with static allocation buffers
- Generates separate CSV output in `unit_performance/libnn.csv`
- Different output directory structure (`t_pf_*.c` tests)

#### **Code Size Measurement** (ENA_MEASURE_CODE_SIZE=1)
- Measures per-function code size across sections: text, code, rodata, data, bss
- Outputs to CSV: `fun_name,text,code,rodata,data,bss`
- Disables code coverage checking when enabled

#### **Golden Data Dumping** (ENA_DUMP_GOLDEN=1)
- Generates reference golden files instead of comparing against them
- Used for creating test datasets
- Only works with dynamic allocation buffer tests

#### **Random Input Testing** (ENA_RANDOM_INPUT=1)
- Generates random inputs instead of reading from files
- Used in environments without file I/O support (e.g., M4, M7)
- Results not verified against golden data

#### **Parameter Extraction** (ENA_GREP_PARAM_TO_CSV=1)
- Collects test parameters into CSV format
- Outputs: `group, function, parameters`

#### **CMSIS-NN Wrapper Verification** (ENA_VERIFY_CMSIS_NN_WRAPPER=1)
- Validates compatibility wrapper between Andes-NN and CMSIS-NN APIs

### 4. APPLICATION/MODEL TESTS

Configurable in project-specific `run.sh` scripts (e.g., `/local/nick/libnn/project/ax45mpv/run.sh`):

#### **Individual Application Tests:**
- **CIFAR10** - Image classification (ENA_RUN_CIFAR10=1)
- **GRU** - Recurrent neural network (ENA_RUN_GRU=1)
- **PNET** - P-Net model from MTCNN (ENA_RUN_PNET=1)
- **MTCNN** - Multi-task cascaded CNNs (ENA_RUN_MTCNN=1)
- **MTCNN_DW** - MTCNN variant with depthwise (ENA_RUN_MTCNN_DW=1)
- **DW_TEST** - Depthwise convolution tests (ENA_RUN_DW_TEST=1)
- **DW_NONSQ_TEST** - Non-square depthwise tests (ENA_RUN_DW_NONSQ_TEST=1)

**Application Test Outputs:**
- Binary executables (`.adx` files)
- Memory maps (`.map` files)
- Disassembly files (`.objdump`)
- Size information (`.size`)
- Build logs (`.log`)
- Accuracy comparison logs

### 5. CODE COVERAGE TESTING

Only in DAILY mode, only on simulators (SID/QEMU), not on BOARD:

- Uses gcov/gcda files to measure function coverage
- Generates coverage report: `coverage.csv` with per-function coverage percentages
- Identifies coverage failures (functions not reaching 100% coverage)
- Creates summary report of failed coverage functions
- Note: 100% coverage impossible for some library functions due to simulator limitations

### 6. PERFORMANCE PROFILING

Multiple levels:

#### **Unit Function Level**
- Instruction count (inst)
- Cycle count via performance counter
- Cache/miss stall counts (optionally)

#### **Application Level**
- Function-level profiling (FUNCTION_PF_ANALYZE)
- Application-level profiling (default when PF_COUNTER enabled)

#### **Environment Setup**
- `ENA_CACHEABLE_ENV_SETUP` - For v-extension with cacheable memory
- `ENA_RUN_TWICE` - Warmup run + measured run to stabilize cache state
- `ENA_DUMP_PARAMETERS` - Captures test parameters to CSV (used by test_perf.sh)

### 7. SPECIAL TEST CONFIGURATIONS

Configurable in project `run.sh`:

#### **Vector Extension Options** (ENA_VEC_ISA=1)
- **VEC_CONFIG**: zve32x, zve32f, zve64x, zve64f, or zve64d
- **ENA_VDOT**: Enable/disable dot product extension
- **ENA_VEC_INTRIN**: Intrinsic vs inline assembly implementations
- **ENA_ZVLSSEG**: Vector segmented load/store support
- **ENA_VL4**: 4-bit vector length support

#### **Execution Targets**
- BOARD - Real hardware execution
- SID - Simulator execution with detailed statistics
- QEMU - QEMU emulator with vector extension variants
- Different QEMU configs: v128_d128, v256_d256, v512_d512, v1024_d1024 (V-length and DLEN)

#### **Compiler Options**
- GCC vs Clang selection
- HVM (Heterogeneous VM) support
- HPM (Hardware Performance Monitor) support
- ACE-RVV (Andes Custom Extension) support

### 8. OUTPUT AND REPORTING

Test execution generates structured output in timestamped directory `test_sh_YYYYMMDDHHMMSS/`:

```
test_sh_YYYYMMDDHHMMSS/
├── log/                           # Individual test logs
│   ├── t_activate_s8.log
│   ├── t_conv_HWC_s8_...log
│   └── ...
├── adx/                           # Compiled executables
│   ├── t_activate_s8.adx
│   └── ...
├── objdump/                       # Disassembly files
│   ├── t_activate_s8.objdump
│   └── ...
├── gcov/                          # Temporary gcov outputs (DAILY only)
├── faild_gcov/                    # Failed coverage gcov files
├── performance.csv                # Performance metrics
├── parameter.csv                  # Test parameters (optional)
├── size.csv                       # Code size per function (optional)
├── coverage.csv                   # Code coverage per function (DAILY only)
├── accuracy_fail_list             # Functions that failed accuracy test
├── test_sh_YYYYMMDDHHMMSS.log     # Main test execution log
└── diff_log                       # Error summary (created if failures exist)
```

### 9. TEST_PERF.SH - DEDICATED PERFORMANCE TESTING

`test_perf.sh` is a wrapper that calls `test.sh` with pre-configured CPU flags and performance measurement enabled:

**Input:**
- CPU name (N225, D25, AX25, NX45, AX45MPV, etc.)
- Compiler (riscv32-elf-gcc/clang or riscv64-elf-gcc/clang)
- Target (BOARD only)

**CPU-Specific Features:**
- Auto-selects appropriate DSP, vector, and optimization flags
- Configures library variants (lnn, lnn_v, lnn_v_elen64, etc.)
- Selects correct simulator wrapper per CPU architecture
- Auto-enables zfh (f16) extension when supported

**Output:**
Moves performance results to timestamped test directory with focus on performance.csv

### 10. SUMMARY STATISTICS

All test modes generate final summary including:
- Total functions tested
- Executed functions count
- Pass rate percentage
- Unsupported functions (e.g., f16 without zfh)
- Failed accuracy functions (with detailed list)
- Failed coverage functions (with percentages)
- Execution time
- All output log paths

---

**Key Absolute Paths:**

- Test main script: `/local/nick/libnn/test.sh` (1290 lines)
- Performance testing: `/local/nick/libnn/test_perf.sh`
- Unit tests source: `/local/nick/libnn/Examples/unit_func/` (302 test files)
- Unit test runner: `/local/nick/libnn/Examples/unit_func/run.sh`
- Performance test sources: `/local/nick/libnn/Examples/unit_performance/` (static buffer tests)
- Application tests: `/local/nick/libnn/Examples/{CIFAR10,ARM,ML-KWS-for-MCU,ML-MTCNN,ML-PNET,MTCNN_PRDEPTH,dw_test,dw_nonsq_test}/`
- Project configs: `/local/nick/libnn/project/*/run.sh` (28 CPU architectures)

### 1. Accuracy Tests (`test.sh`)

- **DAILY** — rebuilds library from source, tests accuracy + code coverage (on SID/QEMU only)

Runs ~302 unit test files (`Examples/unit_func/t_*.c`) across 9 categories: activation, basic, concatenation, convolution, fully-connected, pooling, softmax, util, and support functions.

Measures instruction and cycle counts per function. Runs on BOARD only. Outputs results to CSV. Uses `-DENA_RUN_TWICE` for cache warmup + measured run.

Uses gcov to measure per-function coverage. Flags functions below 100%. Only works on simulators (SID/QEMU), not on BOARD.

Measures text/code/rodata/data/bss per function by diffing against an empty-stub build.

For AX45MPV with QEMU, the most relevant are **accuracy tests** (BS3 or DAILY via `test.sh`) and **application tests** (via `project/ax45mpv/run.sh`). Performance profiling requires BOARD or can be approximated with QEMU using the `FUNCTION_PF_ANALYZE` flag in `run.sh`.

# libnn — Andes Neural Network Library

## Build (AX45MPV)


content.text:
`ENA_VL4` enables **4-bit integer (`s4`) function tests** when using the vector extension. 

VL4 refers to the ability to process 4-bit elements with RVV (effectively doubling the number of elements per vector register vs 8-bit). When `ENA_VL4=0` and vector is enabled, all `_s4` tests are skipped. In `project/ax45mpv/run.sh` it's set to `ENA_VL4=1` by default.

Not really — they serve different purposes:

**`run.sh`** (project/ax45mpv):
- Builds library + runs apps/unit tests in one go
- Configurable switches (QEMU/SID/BOARD, vector configs, profiling modes)
- Calls `Examples/unit_func/run.sh` directly for unit tests
- No code coverage, no DAILY/BS3 modes
- Focused on development iteration and profiling

**`test.sh`**:
- DAILY mode rebuilds library + accuracy + **code coverage** (gcov)
- BS3 mode uses pre-built multilib
- Generates structured reports (coverage CSV, size CSV, fail lists)
- Handles special RVV coverage cases across multiple simulator wrappers
- Used by CI / release validation

The key thing `run.sh` can't do is **code coverage**. If you don't need coverage, `run.sh` is actually more convenient for AX45MPV+QEMU — it has all the switches pre-configured and you just toggle flags.

For your day-to-day work, `run.sh` is probably sufficient. Use `test.sh DAILY` when you need the coverage report.

input.content:
# libnn — Andes Neural Network Library

## Build (AX45MPV)

```bash
export PATH=/local/nick/SW_Release_cp/ast530/nds64le-elf-newlib-v5d/bin/:$PATH
./build.sh AX45MPV
```

Output: `libnn.a` in the repository root.

The library is VLEN-agnostic — the same binary runs on any VLEN. `MAX_VLEN=1024` is only used for compile-time buffer sizing; actual vector length is determined at runtime via `vsetvl`.

## Test (AX45MPV + QEMU)

There are two test scripts. Use `run.sh` for day-to-day work; use `test.sh DAILY` when you need code coverage.

| | `run.sh` | `test.sh DAILY` |
|---|---|---|
| Rebuilds library | yes | yes |
| Unit tests (~302) | yes | yes |
| App tests (CIFAR10, MTCNN, ...) | yes | no |
| Code coverage (gcov) | no | yes |
| Profiling switches | yes | no |

### run.sh (daily development)

Builds the library, runs unit tests and application tests on QEMU.

```bash
export PATH=/local/nick/libnn:/local/nick/SW_Release_cp/ast530/nds64le-elf-newlib-v5d/bin/:$PATH
export QEMU_SYS_RV64=/local/nick/qemu_andes/build/qemu-system-riscv64

cd project/ax45mpv
./run.sh
```

Note: The repo root must be in `PATH` because `run.sh` calls `build.sh` without a path.

message.content: how to get the cycle for all functions?

content.text:
From `project/ax45mpv/run.sh`, it's already configured for function-level profiling:

```
APP_RUN_CONFIG=2    # enables -DFAST_VERSION -DFUNCTION_PF_ANALYZE
```

And `COMMON_CFLAG` includes `-DPF_COUNTER` (line 66).

With `ENA_RUN_UNIT_FUNC=1`, it calls `Examples/unit_func/run.sh` which collects inst/cycle counts per function into a CSV.

Let me check how that CSV is generated.

content.content:
1→#!/usr/bin/env bash
     2→################################################################################
     3→# Input
     4→# $1: toolcahin prefix
     5→# $2: the wrapper to execute the application
     6→# $3: extra flags
     7→################################################################################
     8→
     9→#######################################################
    10→# run the unit test and check the accuracy results
    11→#   $1: test function name
    12→#   $2: test function group name (for grouping excel results)
    13→#######################################################
    14→function run_and_check {
    15→    # skip the fp16 functions when the zfh extension is not enabled
    16→    if [[ ${ENA_FP16} -eq 0 ]] && [[ "${1}" == *"f16"* ]]; then
    17→        unsupport_num=$((unsupport_num + 1))
    18→
    19→        if [ ${ENA_GREP_PF_TO_CSV} == 1 ]; then
    20→            echo "$2,$1,x,x," >> ${CSV_OUT}
    21→        fi
    22→        return 0
    23→    fi
    24→
    25→    # skip the integer 4-bit functions when the v-ext enabled but the VL4 not enabled
    26→    if [[ ${CFLAGS} =~ -mext-vector ]] && [[ ${ENA_VL4} -eq 0 ]] && [[ "${1}" == *"s4"* ]]; then
    27→        unsupport_num=$((unsupport_num + 1))
    28→
    29→        if [ ${ENA_GREP_PF_TO_CSV} == 1 ]; then
    30→            echo "$2,$1,x,x," >> ${CSV_OUT}
    31→        fi
    32→        return 0
    33→    fi
    34→
    35→    echo "$1"
    36→    log="${LOG_DIR}/${1}.log"
    37→    build_cmd="${CC} ${CFLAGS} ${MACRO} ${INC_DIR} -o ${ADX_DIR}/${TEST_SOURCE_PREFIX}${1}.adx ${TEST_SOURCE_DIR}/${TEST_SOURCE_PREFIX}${1}.c ${LINKED_LIB} -lm"
    38→    set -x
    39→        ${build_cmd}
    40→        local ret=${?}  #check the compiling successful or not
    41→        if [ ${ret} == 0 ]; then
    42→            # dump the disassembly
    43→            ${OBJDUMP} "${ADX_DIR}/${TEST_SOURCE_PREFIX}${1}.adx" -S > "${OBJDUMP_DIR}/${TEST_SOURCE_PREFIX}${1}.objdump"
    44→
    45→            echo "Start at $(date)"
    46→            ${SIM_WRAPPER} ${ADX_DIR}/${TEST_SOURCE_PREFIX}${1}.adx 2>&1 | tee ${log}
    47→            ret=${PIPESTATUS[0]}
    48→            echo "End at $(date)"
    49→        fi
    50→    set +x
    51→
    52→    if [ ${ret} == 0 ]; then
    53→        # check whehter the program is executed (For the case of AndeSim
    54→        # doesn't launch successfully. BTW, AndeSim doesn't return error
    55→        # code even if it enounters some issues.)
    56→        # Here we use the ALWAYS present string as the criteria whether

#!/usr/bin/env bash
################################################################################
# Input
# $1: toolcahin prefix
# $2: the wrapper to execute the application
# $3: extra flags
################################################################################

#######################################################
# run the unit test and check the accuracy results
#   $1: test function name
#   $2: test function group name (for grouping excel results)
#######################################################
function run_and_check {
    # skip the fp16 functions when the zfh extension is not enabled
    if [[ ${ENA_FP16} -eq 0 ]] && [[ "${1}" == *"f16"* ]]; then
        unsupport_num=$((unsupport_num + 1))

        if [ ${ENA_GREP_PF_TO_CSV} == 1 ]; then
            echo "$2,$1,x,x," >> ${CSV_OUT}
        fi
        return 0
    fi

    # skip the integer 4-bit functions when the v-ext enabled but the VL4 not enabled
    if [[ ${CFLAGS} =~ -mext-vector ]] && [[ ${ENA_VL4} -eq 0 ]] && [[ "${1}" == *"s4"* ]]; then
        unsupport_num=$((unsupport_num + 1))

        if [ ${ENA_GREP_PF_TO_CSV} == 1 ]; then
            echo "$2,$1,x,x," >> ${CSV_OUT}
        fi
        return 0
    fi

    echo "$1"
    log="${LOG_DIR}/${1}.log"
    build_cmd="${CC} ${CFLAGS} ${MACRO} ${INC_DIR} -o ${ADX_DIR}/${TEST_SOURCE_PREFIX}${1}.adx ${TEST_SOURCE_DIR}/${TEST_SOURCE_PREFIX}${1}.c ${LINKED_LIB} -lm"
    set -x
        ${build_cmd}
        local ret=${?}  #check the compiling successful or not
        if [ ${ret} == 0 ]; then
            # dump the disassembly
            ${OBJDUMP} "${ADX_DIR}/${TEST_SOURCE_PREFIX}${1}.adx" -S > "${OBJDUMP_DIR}/${TEST_SOURCE_PREFIX}${1}.objdump"

            echo "Start at $(date)"
            ${SIM_WRAPPER} ${ADX_DIR}/${TEST_SOURCE_PREFIX}${1}.adx 2>&1 | tee ${log}
            ret=${PIPESTATUS[0]}
            echo "End at $(date)"
        fi
    set +x

    if [ ${ret} == 0 ]; then
        # check whehter the program is executed (For the case of AndeSim
        # doesn't launch successfully. BTW, AndeSim doesn't return error
        # code even if it enounters some issues.)
        # Here we use the ALWAYS present string as the criteria whether
        # the test program is executed.
        grep -w 'pass\|fail' ${log} &> /dev/null
        if [ $? -ne 0 ]; then
            fail_list+=("${1}")
            echo "Warning! There is no 'pass' or 'fail' string is parsed in this log file; therefore, the program may not executed." >> ${log}
        else
            pass_num=$((pass_num + 1))
        fi

        if [ ${ENA_GREP_PF_TO_CSV} == 1 ]; then
            #dump inst/cycle into csv
            echo -e "$2,$1,\c" >> ${CSV_OUT}
            grep -w "inst" ${log} | awk '{printf "%ld,", $5}' >> ${CSV_OUT}
            grep -w "cycle count" ${log} | awk '{printf "%ld,", $5}' >> ${CSV_OUT}
            #grep -w "miss stall" ${log} | awk '{printf "%ld,", $7}' >> ${CSV_OUT}
            echo >> ${CSV_OUT}  #new line
        fi
    else
        fail_list+=("${1}")
    fi
}

################################################################################
#                                   main                                       #

content.text:
The cycle collection is controlled by `Examples/unit_func/run.sh`, not by `project/ax45mpv/run.sh` directly. The key flags in `Examples/unit_func/run.sh` are:

```
ENA_MEASURE_PERF=0          # use large test cases for performance
ENA_GREP_PF_TO_CSV=0        # grep inst/cycle into csv
```

Both are **off by default**. To get cycles for all functions, edit `Examples/unit_func/run.sh`:

```
ENA_MEASURE_PERF=1
ENA_GREP_PF_TO_CSV=1
```

Then run `project/ax45mpv/run.sh` as before. The output CSV goes to `Examples/unit_func/libnn.csv` with format:

```
group, function, inst, cycle
```

The `-DPF_COUNTER` flag is already set in `run.sh`'s `COMMON_CFLAG`, so the test programs will print inst/cycle counts — `ENA_GREP_PF_TO_CSV` just controls whether those get collected into the CSV.

Want me to add this to the README?

# libnn — Andes Neural Network Library

## Build (AX45MPV)

```bash
export PATH=/local/nick/SW_Release_cp/ast530/nds64le-elf-newlib-v5d/bin/:$PATH
./build.sh AX45MPV
```

Output: `libnn.a` in the repository root.

The library is VLEN-agnostic — the same binary runs on any VLEN. `MAX_VLEN=1024` is only used for compile-time buffer sizing; actual vector length is determined at runtime via `vsetvl`.

## Test (AX45MPV + QEMU)

Builds the library, runs unit tests and application tests on QEMU.

```bash
export PATH=/local/nick/libnn:/local/nick/SW_Release_cp/ast530/nds64le-elf-newlib-v5d/bin/:$PATH
export QEMU_SYS_RV64=/local/nick/qemu_andes/build/qemu-system-riscv64

cd project/ax45mpv
./run.sh
```

Note: The repo root must be in `PATH` because `run.sh` calls `build.sh` without a path.

Edit `run.sh` to select what to run:

```
ENA_RUN_CIFAR10=0       # image classification
ENA_RUN_GRU=0           # recurrent network
ENA_RUN_PNET=0          # P-Net (MTCNN stage)
ENA_RUN_MTCNN=0         # multi-task cascaded CNN
ENA_RUN_UNIT_FUNC=1     # unit function tests (default on)
RUN_CONFIG=2            # 0=BOARD, 1=SID, 2=QEMU
```

```

## Profiling — Cycle Counts (AX45MPV + QEMU)

To collect inst/cycle counts for all functions, edit `Examples/unit_func/run.sh`:

```
ENA_MEASURE_PERF=1
ENA_GREP_PF_TO_CSV=1
```

Then run `project/ax45mpv/run.sh` as above. Results are written to `Examples/unit_func/libnn.csv`:

```
group, function, inst, cycle

#!/bin/bash
#set -x

OLD_PWD=`pwd`
ADX_PATH="$( cd "$( dirname "$1" )" && echo `pwd`/$(basename "$1"))"
THIS_DIR="$( cd "$( dirname "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}" )" && pwd )"

# This file is a board simulator wrapper shell script for both nds32le-elf & nds32be-elf,
# which is called during the gcc/plumhall/supertest testsuite verification flow.
#
# Currently, the nds32 simulator -- run binary on board -- does not run program in intuitive way.
# The simulatior requires a gdb expect file in which the execution file is strictly specified.
# We use this wrapper script to complete the work so that the testsuite can easily use it to do simulation.

# this value should be replaced by build system when start testing
SIMULATOR_DIR=${THIS_DIR}

# check whether MYPC environment variable is set up
if [ -z ${MYPC} ]; then
    echo
    echo "Warning! MYPC environment variable (where ICEman launches on) is not set yet."
    echo "GDB will take localhost as the target remote."
    echo
fi

# check whether MYPORT environment variable is set up
if [ -z ${MYPORT} ]; then
    echo
    echo "Error! MYPORT environment variable is not set yet."
    echo "Please set MYPORT to the socket port number of ICEman for GDB connection."
    echo
    exit 1
fi

# the following variables will combine directory name and file name to form a full-path
RUN_GDB=$SIMULATOR_DIR/gdb-ice-riscv64.exp

# check if the execution file is available
if [ "$1" = "" ] || [ ! -f "$1" ]; then
    echo ""
    echo "No such file $1"
    echo ""
    exit 1
fi

# get the executable file
RUN_FILE=$ADX_PATH

# set gcc testsuite should be "yes" or "no", it will effect how to get return value.
GCC="no"

# set the board cache will be "on" or "lm". lm measns using local memory.
CACHE="on"

# set the board need reset-and-run or not.
RESET_BOARD="on"

# setup the PMA for HVM (it could be "on" or "off")
HVM_PMA="off"

# setup the DDR latency (it could be "on" or "off")
DDR_LATENCY="on"

# enable the non-blocking load (it could be "on" or "off")
NON_BLOCKING_LOAD="on"

# setup the D$ write-around threshold (it could be 0~3)
DC_WA_THRESHOLD="1"

# we should reset the board when we run first test case in every testsuite.
if [ ! -f "$SIMULATOR_DIR/First_Reset_done" ]; then
    RESET="on"
    touch $SIMULATOR_DIR/First_Reset_done
else
    RESET=$RESET_BOARD
fi

# physically invoke simulator
if test -r CVALSTDIN
then
	expect $RUN_GDB $RUN_FILE $GCC $CACHE $RESET $HVM_PMA $DDR_LATENCY $NON_BLOCKING_LOAD $DC_WA_THRESHOLD $CVALSTDIN
else
	expect $RUN_GDB $RUN_FILE $GCC $CACHE $RESET $HVM_PMA $DDR_LATENCY $NON_BLOCKING_LOAD $DC_WA_THRESHOLD
fi
# Note. Please do NOT add any command between expect $RUN_GDB blah blah blah... and ret=$? to make
# sure the scirpt can get correct return value from GDB.
ret=$?

cd ${OLD_PWD}

exit ${ret}

In `project/ax45mpv/run.sh`, set `RUN_CONFIG=0` for board, then:

```bash
export PATH=/local/nick/libnn:/local/nick/SW_Release_cp/ast530/nds64le-elf-newlib-v5d/bin/:$PATH
export MYPC=sw-boards.andestech.com
export MYPORT=1116

cd project/ax45mpv
./run.sh
```

file.content:
# libnn — Andes Neural Network Library

## Build (AX45MPV)

```bash
export PATH=/local/nick/SW_Release_cp/ast530/nds64le-elf-newlib-v5d/bin/:$PATH
./build.sh AX45MPV
```

Output: `libnn.a` in the repository root.

The library is VLEN-agnostic — the same binary runs on any VLEN. `MAX_VLEN=1024` is only used for compile-time buffer sizing; actual vector length is determined at runtime via `vsetvl`.

## Test (AX45MPV + QEMU)

Builds the library, runs unit tests and application tests on QEMU.

```bash
export PATH=/local/nick/libnn:/local/nick/SW_Release_cp/ast530/nds64le-elf-newlib-v5d/bin/:$PATH
export QEMU_SYS_RV64=/local/nick/qemu_andes/build/qemu-system-riscv64

cd project/ax45mpv
./run.sh
```

Note: The repo root must be in `PATH` because `run.sh` calls `build.sh` without a path.

Edit `run.sh` to select what to run:

```
ENA_RUN_CIFAR10=0       # image classification
ENA_RUN_GRU=0           # recurrent network
ENA_RUN_PNET=0          # P-Net (MTCNN stage)
ENA_RUN_MTCNN=0         # multi-task cascaded CNN
ENA_RUN_UNIT_FUNC=1     # unit function tests (default on)
RUN_CONFIG=2            # 0=BOARD, 1=SID, 2=QEMU
```

## Profiling — Cycle Counts (AX45MPV + QEMU)

To collect inst/cycle counts for all functions, edit `Examples/unit_func/run.sh`:

```
ENA_MEASURE_PERF=1
ENA_GREP_PF_TO_CSV=1
```

Then run `project/ax45mpv/run.sh` as above. Results are written to `Examples/unit_func/libnn.csv`:

```
group, function, inst, cycle
```

```bash
export MYPC=sw-boards.andestech.com
```

#!/usr/bin/env bash
################################################################################
# Input
# $1: toolcahin prefix
# $2: the wrapper to execute the application
# $3: extra flags
################################################################################

#######################################################
# run the unit test and check the accuracy results
#   $1: test function name
#   $2: test function group name (for grouping excel results)
#######################################################
function run_and_check {
    # skip the fp16 functions when the zfh extension is not enabled
    if [[ ${ENA_FP16} -eq 0 ]] && [[ "${1}" == *"f16"* ]]; then
        unsupport_num=$((unsupport_num + 1))

        if [ ${ENA_GREP_PF_TO_CSV} == 1 ]; then
            echo "$2,$1,x,x," >> ${CSV_OUT}
        fi
        return 0
    fi

    # skip the integer 4-bit functions when the v-ext enabled but the VL4 not enabled
    if [[ ${CFLAGS} =~ -mext-vector ]] && [[ ${ENA_VL4} -eq 0 ]] && [[ "${1}" == *"s4"* ]]; then
        unsupport_num=$((unsupport_num + 1))

        if [ ${ENA_GREP_PF_TO_CSV} == 1 ]; then
            echo "$2,$1,x,x," >> ${CSV_OUT}
        fi
        return 0
    fi

    echo "$1"
    log="${LOG_DIR}/${1}.log"
    build_cmd="${CC} ${CFLAGS} ${MACRO} ${INC_DIR} -o ${ADX_DIR}/${TEST_SOURCE_PREFIX}${1}.adx ${TEST_SOURCE_DIR}/${TEST_SOURCE_PREFIX}${1}.c ${LINKED_LIB} -lm"
    set -x
        ${build_cmd}
        local ret=${?}  #check the compiling successful or not
        if [ ${ret} == 0 ]; then
            # dump the disassembly
            ${OBJDUMP} "${ADX_DIR}/${TEST_SOURCE_PREFIX}${1}.adx" -S > "${OBJDUMP_DIR}/${TEST_SOURCE_PREFIX}${1}.objdump"

            echo "Start at $(date)"
            ${SIM_WRAPPER} ${ADX_DIR}/${TEST_SOURCE_PREFIX}${1}.adx 2>&1 | tee ${log}
            ret=${PIPESTATUS[0]}
            echo "End at $(date)"
        fi
    set +x

    if [ ${ret} == 0 ]; then
        # check whehter the program is executed (For the case of AndeSim
        # doesn't launch successfully. BTW, AndeSim doesn't return error
        # code even if it enounters some issues.)
        # Here we use the ALWAYS present string as the criteria whether
        # the test program is executed.
        grep -w 'pass\|fail' ${log} &> /dev/null
        if [ $? -ne 0 ]; then
            fail_list+=("${1}")
            echo "Warning! There is no 'pass' or 'fail' string is parsed in this log file; therefore, the program may not executed." >> ${log}
        else
            pass_num=$((pass_num + 1))
        fi

        if [ ${ENA_GREP_PF_TO_CSV} == 1 ]; then
            #dump inst/cycle into csv
            echo -e "$2,$1,\c" >> ${CSV_OUT}
            grep -w "inst" ${log} | awk '{printf "%ld,", $5}' >> ${CSV_OUT}
            grep -w "cycle count" ${log} | awk '{printf "%ld,", $5}' >> ${CSV_OUT}
            #grep -w "miss stall" ${log} | awk '{printf "%ld,", $7}' >> ${CSV_OUT}
            echo >> ${CSV_OUT}  #new line
        fi
    else
        fail_list+=("${1}")
    fi
}

################################################################################
#                                   main                                       #
################################################################################
ENA_TEST_ALL=1
ENA_ACTIVATION_TEST=0
ENA_BASIC_TEST=0
ENA_CONCATE_TEST=0
ENA_CONV_TEST=0
ENA_FC_TEST=0
ENA_POOLING_TEST=0
ENA_SOFTMAX_TEST=0
ENA_UTIL_TEST=0

ENA_DUMP_GOLDEN=0           # only for the test programs with DYNAMIC allocation buffers
ENA_MEASURE_PERF=0          # only run large test case to measure the performance (
                            # run the program with DYNAMIC allocation buffers)
ENA_GREP_PF_TO_CSV=0        # grep instruction and cycle counts into csv
ENA_MEASURE_PERF_STATIC=0   # run the test prgram with STATIC allocation buffers
ENA_RANDOM_INPUT=0          # The testers can turn on this switch as running the
                            # test programs in the environment not supporting
                            # FILE I/O, e.g. m4 or m7. The inputs will be randomly
                            # generated and the results will not be verified.
                            # (this switch is ONLY for STATIC allocation buffer tests)
ENA_VERIFY_CMSIS_NN_WRAPPER=0   # verify the wrapper header file between andes-nn and cmsis-nn

# Since strided load is not supported now. If vector extension
# is enabled, those functions use strided load won't be executed.
# if [[ "${CFLAGS}" == *"-mext-vector"* ]] && [[ "${CFLAGS}" == *"-mvh"* ]]; then
#     ENA_UNIT_STRIDE_ONLY=1      #only run the test cases with unit stride acess
# else
    ENA_UNIT_STRIDE_ONLY=0
# fi

#===== configure test environment_begin =====
CC=${1}
if [[ $CC =~ - ]]; then
    OBJDUMP="${CC%-*}-objdump"
else
    OBJDUMP="objdump"
fi
SIM_WRAPPER=${2}
EXTRA_FLAG="${3}"
CFLAGS="-Wall -Werror -O3 ${EXTRA_FLAG}"
MACRO="-DENA_DUMP_GOLDEN=${ENA_DUMP_GOLDEN} -DENA_MEASURE_PERF=${ENA_MEASURE_PERF} -DENA_RANDOM_INPUT=${ENA_RANDOM_INPUT}"
UNIT_TEST_PATH=$(readlink -f `dirname ${0}`)
UNIT_PERFORMANCE_PATH="${UNIT_TEST_PATH}/../unit_performance"
INC_DIR="-I${UNIT_TEST_PATH}/../../Include/ -I${UNIT_TEST_PATH}/../../libhvm-project/libhvm/include/ -I${UNIT_TEST_PATH}/include/"
LINKED_LIB="${UNIT_TEST_PATH}/../../libnn.a"
if [ ${ENA_MEASURE_PERF_STATIC} -eq 1 ]; then
    CSV_OUT="${UNIT_PERFORMANCE_PATH}/libnn.csv"
    LOG_DIR="${UNIT_PERFORMANCE_PATH}/log"
    ADX_DIR="${UNIT_PERFORMANCE_PATH}/adx"
    OBJDUMP_DIR="${UNIT_PERFORMANCE_PATH}/objdump"
    TEST_SOURCE_DIR="${UNIT_PERFORMANCE_PATH}"
    TEST_SOURCE_PREFIX="t_pf_"
else    #ENA_MEASURE_PERF_STATIC
    CSV_OUT="${UNIT_TEST_PATH}/libnn.csv"
    LOG_DIR="${UNIT_TEST_PATH}/log"
    ADX_DIR="${UNIT_TEST_PATH}/adx"
    OBJDUMP_DIR="${UNIT_TEST_PATH}/objdump"
    TEST_SOURCE_DIR="${UNIT_TEST_PATH}"
    TEST_SOURCE_PREFIX="t_"
fi
if [ ${ENA_VERIFY_CMSIS_NN_WRAPPER} -eq 1 ]; then
    INC_DIR+=" -I${UNIT_TEST_PATH}/../../cmsis_include"
    MACRO+=" -DENA_VERIFY_CMSIS_NN_WRAPPER"
fi
if [[ "${CFLAGS}" == *"-mext-vector"* ]]; then
    # As using the v-ext, now the fast algorithm will be applied by default for
    # some floating-piont functions.
    MACRO+=" -DENA_FAST_ALGO"

    # As measuring v-ext algorithm on cacheable environment, we can enable
    # ENA_CACHEABLE_ENV_SETUP switch to get better performance.
    MACRO+=" -DENA_CACHEABLE_ENV_SETUP"
fi
if [[ ${CFLAGS} =~ ENA_HVM ]]; then
    LINKED_LIB+=" ${UNIT_TEST_PATH}/../../libhvm.a"
fi
#===== configure test environment_end =====

# create folders and clean old files
mkdir -p ${ADX_DIR}
mkdir -p ${OBJDUMP_DIR}
mkdir -p ${LOG_DIR}
rm -f ${LOG_DIR}/*.log          # clean old logs
rm -f ${ADX_DIR}/*.adx          # clean old executables
rm -f ${OBJDUMP_DIR}/*.objdump  # clean old objdump files
#init global variable
pass_num=0
unsupport_num=0

ACTIVATION=(
    "activate_s8"
    "activate_s16"
    "activate_s16_hp"
    "hardswish_s8"
    "leaky_relu_s8"
    "leaky_relu_s8_asym"
    "leaky_relu_s16_asym"
    "prelu_s8_asym"
    "relu_s8"
    "relu_s16"
    "relu_any_s8"
    "relu_any_s16"
    "sigmoid_s8"
    "sigmoid_s16"
    "silu_s8"
    "tanh_s8"
    "tanh_s16"

    "activate_s8_2buf"
    "activate_s16_2buf"
    "leaky_relu_s8_2buf"
    "relu_s8_2buf"
    "relu_s16_2buf"
    "relu_any_s8_2buf"

    "gelu_f16"
    "gelu_f32"
    "leaky_relu_f16"
    "relu_f16"
    "relu_any_f16"
    "sigmoid_f16"
    "silu_f16"
    "silu_f32"
    "swish_f16"
    "swish_f32"

BASIC=(

)

if [ ${ENA_TEST_ALL} == 1 ] || [ ${ENA_ACTIVATION_TEST} == 1 ]; then
    test_list+=("${ACTIVATION[@]/%/:activation}")
fi
if [ ${ENA_TEST_ALL} == 1 ] || [ ${ENA_BASIC_TEST} == 1 ]; then
    test_list+=("${BASIC[@]/%/:basic}")
fi
if [ ${ENA_TEST_ALL} == 1 ] || [ ${ENA_CONCATE_TEST} == 1 ]; then
    test_list+=("${CONCATE[@]/%/:concatenation}")
fi
if [ ${ENA_TEST_ALL} == 1 ] || [ ${ENA_CONV_TEST} == 1 ]; then
    test_list+=("${CONV[@]/%/:convolution}")
fi
if [ ${ENA_TEST_ALL} == 1 ] || [ ${ENA_FC_TEST} == 1 ]; then
    test_list+=("${FC[@]/%/:fully_connected}")
fi
if [ ${ENA_TEST_ALL} == 1 ] || [ ${ENA_POOLING_TEST} == 1 ]; then
    test_list+=("${POOLING[@]/%/:pooling}")
fi
if [ ${ENA_TEST_ALL} == 1 ] || [ ${ENA_SOFTMAX_TEST} == 1 ]; then
    test_list+=("${SOFTMAX[@]/%/:softmax}")
fi
if [ ${ENA_TEST_ALL} == 1 ] || [ ${ENA_UTIL_TEST} == 1 ]; then
    test_list+=("${UTIL[@]/%/:util}")
fi

#remove non-unit-stride test cases
if [ ${ENA_UNIT_STRIDE_ONLY} == 1 ]; then
    for FUN_NAME in "${NON_UNIT_STRIDE[@]}";
    do
        test_list=(${test_list[@]/$FUN_NAME})
    done
fi

# record whether the zfh extension is enabled
if [[ ${CFLAGS} == *"-mzfh"* ]]; then
    ENA_FP16=1
else
    ENA_FP16=0
fi

# record whether the VL4 extension is enabled
if [[ ${CFLAGS} =~ ENA_VL4 ]]; then
    ENA_VL4=1
else
    ENA_VL4=0
fi

if [ ${ENA_GREP_PF_TO_CSV} == 1 ]; then
    #init csv title
    echo "group, function, inst, cycle" > ${CSV_OUT}
fi

#----- enable the LM switch -----
if [[ ${EXTRA_FLAG} =~ ENA_LM ]]; then
    sed -i 's/CACHE="on"/CACHE="lm"/' ${SIM_WRAPPER}
fi

for item in "${test_list[@]}"; do
    fun_name=$(echo ${item} | cut -d ':' -f1)
    fun_group=$(echo ${item} | cut -d ':' -f2)
    run_and_check "${fun_name}" "${fun_group}"
done

#----- restore the LM switch -----
if [[ ${EXTRA_FLAG} =~ ENA_LM ]]; then
    sed -i 's/CACHE="lm"/CACHE="on"/' ${SIM_WRAPPER}
fi

# only show the execution summary as ENA_DUMP_GOLDEN is disabled
if [ ${ENA_DUMP_GOLDEN} -eq 0 ]; then
    #show result summary
    total_num=${#test_list[@]}
    executed_num="$((total_num - unsupport_num))"
    printf "\n========== accuracy checking summary ==========\n"
    printf "Total functions:       %4d\n" "${#test_list[@]}"
    printf "Executed functions:    %4d\n" "${executed_num}"
    printf "Passed functions:      %4d (pass rate: %.2f%%)\n" "${pass_num}" "$(echo ${pass_num}*100/${executed_num} | bc -l)"
    printf "Unsupported functions: %4d\n" "${unsupport_num}"
fi

# show fail list
if [ ${#fail_list[@]} != 0 ]; then
    echo
    echo "failed funcion list:"
    for FUN_NAME in "${fail_list[@]}"; do
        echo ${FUN_NAME}
    done
fi

toolUseResult.originalFile:
#!/usr/bin/env bash
################################################################################
# Input:
#  $1: extra compilation flags or macro for both libray and application
################################################################################
CWD=$(pwd)

#----- set up the tool name -----
source ../NLIB_CROSS_V5_RV64

#----- build and run switches -----
ENA_BUILD_LIBNN=1
ENA_RUN_CIFAR10=0
ENA_RUN_GRU=0
ENA_RUN_PNET=0
ENA_RUN_MTCNN=0
ENA_RUN_MTCNN_DW=0
ENA_RUN_DW_TEST=0
ENA_RUN_DW_NONSQ_TEST=0
ENA_RUN_UNIT_FUNC=1
RUN_CONFIG=2        #0: run app on board, 1: run app on SID, 2: run app on QEMU
ENA_CLANG=0         #0: use gcc as compiler, 1: use clang as compiler
ENA_RUN_TWICE=0     #0: run function once, 1: run function twice
ENA_VEC_ISA=1       #0: disable v-ext (for measuring plain C algo performance), 1: enable v-ext
ENA_VDOT=1          #0: use the default v-ext algorithm, 1: use vdot v-ext algorithm
ENA_VEC_INTRIN=0    #0: using the inline assembly version v-ext algorithm, 1: use the intrinsic version v-ext algorithm
ENA_HVM=0           #0: place the program's data on cache, 1: place the program's data on HVM
ENA_HPM=0           #0: disable the performance monitor, 1: enable the performance monitor
ENA_ACE_RVV=0       #0: disabe the ACE-RVV, 1: enable the ACE-RVV
ENA_ZVLSSEG=0       #0: disable zvlsseg extension, 1: enable zvlsseg extension
ENA_VL4=1           #0: disable VL4 extension, 1: enable VL4 extension
VEC_CONFIG=4        #0: zve32x, 1: zve32f, 2: zve64x, 3: zve64f, 4: zv64d

#----- flags of library and app -----
CPU_NAME="ax45mpv"
CPU_FLAG="-mtune=andes-45-series"
APP_INCLUDE_PATH="-I./../../Examples/unit_func/include -I./../../Include -I./../../internal"
LIB_INCLUDE_PATH="-I./Include -I./internal"
if [[ ${ENA_VEC_ISA} -eq 1 ]]; then
    if [[ ${VEC_CONFIG} -eq 0 ]]; then
        VEC_CFG="zve32x"
    elif [[ ${VEC_CONFIG} -eq 1 ]]; then
        VEC_CFG="zve32f"
    elif [[ ${VEC_CONFIG} -eq 2 ]]; then
        VEC_CFG="zve64x"
    elif [[ ${VEC_CONFIG} -eq 3 ]]; then
        VEC_CFG="zve64f"
    else
        VEC_CFG="zve64d"
    fi

    CPU_FLAG+=" -mext-vector=${VEC_CFG} -DNDS_VEC_RVV_VERSION=1000"
    # export the VEC_CFG environment variable for selecting the proper QEMU conf
    export VEC_CFG="${VEC_CFG}"
fi
if [[ ${ENA_VEC_ISA} -eq 1 ]] && [[ ${ENA_VDOT} -eq 0 ]]; then
    CPU_FLAG+=" -UENA_NDS_V5_VEC_DOT_PROD"
fi
if [[ ${ENA_VEC_ISA} -eq 1 ]] && [[ ${ENA_ZVLSSEG} -eq 1 ]]; then
    CPU_FLAG+=" -DENA_VEC_ISA_ZVLSSEG"
fi
if [[ ${ENA_VEC_ISA} -eq 1 ]] && [[ ${ENA_CLANG} -eq 1 ]] && [[ ${ENA_VEC_INTRIN} -eq 1 ]]; then
    # please note that now only clang supports RVV intrinsic
    CPU_FLAG+=" -DENA_VEC_INTRINSIC -fno-unroll-loops"
fi
COMMON_CFLAG="${CPU_FLAG} -O3 -Wall -Werror -ffunction-sections -fdata-sections -DPF_COUNTER ${1}"    #shared flags for library ad app
if [[ ${ENA_ACE_RVV} -eq 1 ]]; then
    ACE_LIB_PATH=$(readlink -f ${CWD}/../../ace_lib_exp)
    export -p ACE_LIB_PATH="${ACE_LIB_PATH}"
    COMMON_CFLAG+=" -DENA_ACE_RVV -Wa,-mace=${CWD}/../../ace_lib_exp/libacetool.so"
fi
APP_CFLAG="${COMMON_CFLAG} -Wl,-gc-sections -DENA_FS_VS ${APP_INCLUDE_PATH}"
LIB_CFLAG="${COMMON_CFLAG} -fno-strict-aliasing ${LIB_INCLUDE_PATH}"

# enable mvh
if [[ ${RUN_CONFIG} -eq 0 ]] || [[ ${RUN_CONFIG} -eq 2 ]]; then
    APP_CFLAG="${APP_CFLAG} -mvh"
fi

if [[ ${ENA_RUN_TWICE} -eq 1 ]]; then
    APP_CFLAG="${APP_CFLAG} -DENA_RUN_TWICE"
fi
if [[ ${ENA_HVM} -eq 1 ]]; then
    LIB_CFLAG+=" -mcmodel=large -DENA_HVM"
    APP_CFLAG+=" -mcmodel=large -DENA_HVM"
fi
if [[ ${ENA_HPM} -eq 1 ]]; then
    APP_CFLAG+=" -DENA_CACHE_PFM -DENA_L2C_PFM"
fi

if [[ ${ENA_VL4} -eq 1 ]]; then
    APP_CFLAG+=" -DENA_VL4"
fi

#----- set up the wrapper and compiler  -----
if [[ ${RUN_CONFIG} -eq 2 ]]; then
    # RUN_WRAPPER="riscv-qemu-wrapper-v128_d128_b128"
    # RUN_WRAPPER="riscv-qemu-wrapper-v256_d128_b128"
    # RUN_WRAPPER="riscv-qemu-wrapper-v256_d256_b256"
    # RUN_WRAPPER="riscv-qemu-wrapper-v512_d256_b256"
    RUN_WRAPPER="riscv-qemu-wrapper-v512_d512_b512"
    # RUN_WRAPPER="riscv-qemu-wrapper-v1024_d512_b512"
    # RUN_WRAPPER="riscv-qemu-wrapper-v1024_d1024_b512"
elif [[ ${RUN_CONFIG} -eq 1 ]]; then
    # RUN_WRAPPER="riscv-sim-wrapper-vec_v1_0_v128_d128_b128"
    # RUN_WRAPPER="riscv-sim-wrapper-vec_v1_0_v256_d128_b128"
    # RUN_WRAPPER="riscv-sim-wrapper-vec_v1_0_v256_d256_b256"
    # RUN_WRAPPER="riscv-sim-wrapper-vec_v1_0_v512_d256_b256"
    RUN_WRAPPER="riscv-sim-wrapper-vec_v1_0_v512_d512_b512"
    # RUN_WRAPPER="riscv-sim-wrapper-vec_v1_0_v1024_d512_b512"
    # RUN_WRAPPER="riscv-sim-wrapper-vec_v1_0_v1024_d1024_b512"
else
    RUN_WRAPPER="riscv64-sim-wrapper-on-board"
fi

if [[ ${ENA_CLANG} -eq 1 ]]; then
    CC="${CROSS_COMPILE}-clang"
else
    CC="${CROSS_COMPILE}-gcc"
fi

#----- extra flags -----
# -----
# automatically enable the zfh extension if the toolcahin supports it
# Note. We should check whether the zfh extension could be enabled with
# all compilation flags. The zfh extension may be supported with the
# toolchain; however, it's disabled by some compilation flags.
# -----
# check whether the zfh extension could be enabled
${CC} -mzfh -E -dM ${COMMON_CFLAG} - < /dev/null &> /dev/null
if [ $? -eq 0 ]; then
    # check whether the toolchain is [single|double]-abi
    # (for the spcial case of v5 toolchain + clang)
    ${CC} -mzfh -E -dM ${COMMON_CFLAG} - < /dev/null | grep '__riscv_float_abi_single\|__riscv_float_abi_double' &> /dev/null
    if [ $? -eq 0 ]; then
        APP_CFLAG="${APP_CFLAG} -mzfh"
        LIB_CFLAG="${LIB_CFLAG} -mzfh"
    fi
fi

#----- build the lib -----
if [ ${ENA_BUILD_LIBNN} -eq 1 ];then
    cd ../../
    if [ ${ENA_VEC_ISA} -eq 0 ]; then
        build.sh "NX45" "${LIB_CFLAG}" "${CC}"
    else
        build.sh "${CPU_NAME}" "${LIB_CFLAG}" "${CC}"
    fi
    build_ret=$?
    cd ${CWD}

    if [ ${build_ret} -ne 0 ]; then
        exit 1
    fi
fi



#----- app running configuration -----
# config-0: run app without fast version libnn function and with application level profiling
# config-1: run app with fast version libnn function and application level profiling
# config-2: run app with fast version libnn function and function level profiling
# config-3: run app with printing check sum and debug info
APP_RUN_CONFIG=2

if [ ${APP_RUN_CONFIG} == 0 ]; then
    CFLAGS="${APP_CFLAG}"
elif [ ${APP_RUN_CONFIG} == 1 ]; then
    CFLAGS="${APP_CFLAG} -DFAST_VERSION"
elif [ ${APP_RUN_CONFIG} == 2 ]; then
    CFLAGS="${APP_CFLAG} -DFAST_VERSION -DFUNCTION_PF_ANALYZE"
else
    CFLAGS="${APP_CFLAG} -UPF_COUNTER -DPRINT_GOLDEN -DCHECK -DFAST_VERSION"
fi


#$1: application name
function cmp_result {
    local app=${1}

    #compare the results with goldens
    cmp ${app}.log ../golden/${app}.log
    local ret=$?
    echo
    if [ ${ret} == 0 ] ; then
        echo ">>>>> accuracy checking pass. <<<<<"
    else
        echo ">>>>> accuracy checking FAIL !!! <<<<<"
    fi
    echo
}

if [ ${ENA_RUN_CIFAR10} -eq 1 ];then
    echo
    echo "------------------"
    echo "Running CIFAR10...."
    echo "------------------"
    echo
    make clean_cifar10 -f Makefile_test.mak
    make cifar10 -f Makefile_test.mak CFLAGS="${CFLAGS} -fno-strict-aliasing"
    ${RUN_WRAPPER} cifar10.adx

    if [ ${APP_RUN_CONFIG} == 3 ]; then
        cmp_result "cifar10"
    fi
fi

if [ ${ENA_RUN_GRU} -eq 1 ];then
    echo
    echo "------------------"
    echo "Running GRU...."
    echo "------------------"
    echo
    make clean_gru -f Makefile_test.mak
    make gru -f Makefile_test.mak CFLAGS="${CFLAGS} -fno-strict-aliasing"
    ${RUN_WRAPPER} gru.adx

    if [ ${APP_RUN_CONFIG} == 3 ]; then
        cmp_result "gru"
    fi
fi

if [ ${ENA_RUN_PNET} -eq 1 ];then
    echo
    echo "------------------"
    echo "Running PNET...."
    echo "    Each check-sum MUST be identical with X86."
    echo "------------------"
    echo
    make clean_pnet -f Makefile_test.mak
    make pnet -f Makefile_test.mak CFLAGS="${CFLAGS} -w"
    ${RUN_WRAPPER} pnet.adx

    if [ ${APP_RUN_CONFIG} == 3 ]; then
        cmp_result "pnet"
    fi
fi

if [ ${ENA_RUN_MTCNN} -eq 1 ];then
    echo
    echo "------------------"
    echo "Running MTCNN...."
    echo "------------------"
    echo
    if [ ${RUN_CONFIG} == 0 ];then
        echo "MTCNN cannot be put into local memory; thus, please run it on SID."
    else
        make clean_mtcnn -f Makefile_test.mak
        make mtcnn -f Makefile_test.mak CFLAGS="${CFLAGS} -Wno-unused-variable"
        ${RUN_WRAPPER} mtcnn.adx
    fi

    if [ ${APP_RUN_CONFIG} == 3 ]; then
        cmp_result "mtcnn"
    fi
fi

if [ ${ENA_RUN_MTCNN_DW} -eq 1 ];then
    echo
    echo "------------------"
    echo "Running MTCNN_DW...."
    echo "------------------"
    echo
    if [ ${RUN_CONFIG} == 0 ];then
        echo "MTCNN cannot be put into local memory; thus, please run it on SID."
    else
        make clean -f Makefile_MTCNN_PRDEPTH
        make -f Makefile_MTCNN_PRDEPTH CFLAGS="${CFLAGS} -Wno-unused-variable -Wno-unused-but-set-variable -Wno-maybe-uninitialized"
        ${RUN_WRAPPER} MTCNN_PRDEPTH.adx | tee mtcnn_dw.log
    fi
fi

if [ ${ENA_RUN_DW_TEST} -eq 1 ];then
    echo
    echo "------------------"
    echo "Running DW_TEST...."
    echo "------------------"
    echo
    make clean_dw_test -f Makefile_test.mak
    make dw_test -f Makefile_test.mak CFLAGS="${CFLAGS}"
    ${RUN_WRAPPER} dw_test.adx

    if [ ${APP_RUN_CONFIG} == 3 ]; then
        cmp_result "dw_test"
    fi
fi

if [ ${ENA_RUN_DW_NONSQ_TEST} -eq 1 ];then
    echo
    echo "------------------"
    echo "Running DW_NONSQ_TEST...."
    echo "------------------"
    echo
    make clean_dw_nonsq_test -f Makefile_test.mak
    make dw_nonsq_test -f Makefile_test.mak CFLAGS="${CFLAGS}"
    ${RUN_WRAPPER} dw_nonsq_test.adx

    if [ ${APP_RUN_CONFIG} == 3 ]; then
        cmp_result "dw_nonsq_test"
    fi
fi

if [[ ${ENA_RUN_UNIT_FUNC} -eq 1 ]];then
    echo
    echo "------------------"
    echo "Running unit func...."
    echo "    Checking accuracy and collect the cycle for unit functions."
    echo "------------------"
    echo
    cd ../../Examples/unit_func/
    ./run.sh "${CC}" "${CWD}/${RUN_WRAPPER}" "${APP_CFLAG}"
fi

content.text:
The problem is `riscv64-elf-gdb` can't load `libpython3.4m.so`. GDB fails to start, so no tests actually ran and no cycle data was collected.

The toolchain's GDB depends on `libpython3.4m.so.1.0` which isn't available on this system. You'll need to either:

1. **Install/symlink the missing library** — find and add it to `LD_LIBRARY_PATH`
2. **Use a different GDB** — if you have a working `riscv64-elf-gdb` elsewhere
3. **Skip GDB** — run on QEMU instead (`RUN_CONFIG=2`), which doesn't need GDB

Do you have a working GDB, or should we fall back to QEMU?

file.content:
# libnn — Andes Neural Network Library

## Build (AX45MPV)

```bash
export PATH=/local/nick/SW_Release_cp/ast530/nds64le-elf-newlib-v5d/bin/:$PATH
./build.sh AX45MPV
```

Output: `libnn.a` in the repository root.

The library is VLEN-agnostic — the same binary runs on any VLEN. `MAX_VLEN=1024` is only used for compile-time buffer sizing; actual vector length is determined at runtime via `vsetvl`.

## Test (AX45MPV + QEMU)

Builds the library, runs unit tests and application tests on QEMU.

```bash
export PATH=/local/nick/libnn:/local/nick/SW_Release_cp/ast530/nds64le-elf-newlib-v5d/bin/:$PATH
export QEMU_SYS_RV64=/local/nick/qemu_andes/build/qemu-system-riscv64

cd project/ax45mpv
./run.sh
```

Note: The repo root must be in `PATH` because `run.sh` calls `build.sh` without a path.

Edit `run.sh` to select what to run:

```
ENA_RUN_CIFAR10=0       # image classification
ENA_RUN_GRU=0           # recurrent network
ENA_RUN_PNET=0          # P-Net (MTCNN stage)
ENA_RUN_MTCNN=0         # multi-task cascaded CNN
ENA_RUN_UNIT_FUNC=1     # unit function tests (default on)
RUN_CONFIG=2            # 0=BOARD, 1=SID, 2=QEMU
```

## Test (AX45MPV + FPGA Board)

Same as QEMU but set `RUN_CONFIG=0` in `run.sh` and point to the board via ICEman:

```bash
export PATH=/local/nick/libnn:/local/nick/SW_Release_cp/ast530/nds64le-elf-newlib-v5d/bin/:$PATH
export MYPC=sw-boards.andestech.com
export MYPORT=1116

cd project/ax45mpv
./run.sh
```

## Profiling — Cycle Counts

To collect inst/cycle counts for all functions, edit `Examples/unit_func/run.sh`:

```
ENA_MEASURE_PERF=1
ENA_GREP_PF_TO_CSV=1
```

Then run `project/ax45mpv/run.sh` as above. Results are written to `Examples/unit_func/libnn.csv`:

```
group, function, inst, cycle
```
